On flan-t5-large, adaptation restricted to 128 subspace coordinates matches full LoRA on clean data while holding 62-96% accuracy under label-inversion attacks that collapse LoRA to 3-26%, and its adaptation loss separates clean from corrupted data by 120x without a separate detector.

title: Your Agent's Memories Are Not Its Own: Forged Reasoning Attacks on LLM Agent Memory and Defenses
blurb: This paper introduces FARMA, an attack that poisons an LLM agent's stored reasoning history rather than its factual knowledge, using evasive forged reasoning traces and self-referential reinforcement to defeat keyword and consensus-based defenses at up to a 100% success rate. The authors also present SENTINEL, a layered defense whose Reasoning Guard scores candidate entries on five weighted forgery signals, cutting FARMA's success rate to as low as 0% with no false positives across 326 benign traces.

title: Ternlight – 7 MB embedding model that runs in browser (WASM)
blurb: Ternlight is a 7 MB text embedding model that runs entirely in the browser on CPU via WASM, shipped as a single npm package with no model download step or server call. It embeds text in roughly 5 ms with no network request, and a 5 MB "mini" variant powers in-browser semantic search over document sets like React's docs.

title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
blurb: Forterra deployed more than 100 of its self-driving Lancer ATVs to combat zones in Ukraine over the past nine months, in what it calls the largest fielding of autonomous ground vehicles by a US defense tech company. The gas-powered vehicles, based on Polaris ATVs with a custom sensor and compute stack, carry 750 kilograms of cargo and were modified with Starlink antennas for battlefield use.

title: Sources: Beijing recently held meetings with Alibaba, ByteDance, Z.ai, and others to discuss restricting overseas access to advanced open and closed AI models (Fanny Potkin/Reuters)
blurb: Chinese regulators met with Alibaba, ByteDance, Z.ai, and other domestic AI firms to discuss restricting overseas access to their advanced open and closed models. The move would extend Beijing's export controls into AI, potentially limiting foreign use of Chinese open-weight models that have gained global adoption as lower-cost alternatives.

title: Anthropic's new "J-lens" reveals a silent workspace inside Claude that mirrors a leading theory of consciousness
blurb: Anthropic published a research paper introducing the Jacobian lens (J-lens), an interpretability tool that reveals a "J-space" inside Claude where the model holds concepts it can report on, reason with, and control, atop a much larger layer of automatic processing it cannot access. The 16-author study frames the structure as an analog to global workspace theory and says it has already changed how the company monitors its models for safety risks.

title: North American Startup Funding Shattered Records In First Half Of 2026, Driven By AI
blurb: North American startup funding hit an all-time high of $392 billion in the first half of 2026, driven by late-stage AI megarounds, according to Crunchbase data. Anthropic was the largest fundraiser, pulling in $65 billion at a $965 billion post-money valuation, while capital concentrated in a handful of giant rounds rather than a rising deal count.

title: Bitcoin miner TeraWulf soars on a $19 billion AI data-center lease with Anthropic
blurb: TeraWulf, a former bitcoin miner remade as an AI landlord, signed a 20-year lease with Anthropic to host roughly 401 megawatts of AI data-center capacity at its Kentucky campus starting in 2027. The deal is expected to generate about $19 billion in contracted revenue over its initial term, exceeding TeraWulf's roughly $12 billion market value and sending its shares up as much as 19% on Monday.

title: LLM-as-a-Verifier: A General-Purpose Verification Framework
blurb: This paper introduces LLM-as-a-Verifier, a training-free verification framework that scores agentic solutions by taking the expectation over scoring-token logits to produce continuous rather than discrete judgments. The approach scales along score granularity, repeated evaluation, and criteria decomposition to reach state-of-the-art results on Terminal-Bench V2 (86.5%), SWE-Bench Verified (78.2%), and RoboRewardBench (87.4%), and ships as a Claude Code extension.

title: MoP-JEPA: Hard-Assigned Predictor Mixtures for Stochastic JEPA World Models
blurb: MoP-JEPA replaces the single deterministic predictor in JEPA world models with hard-assigned predictor mixtures, one head per successor mode, to fix a structural collapse where regression-optimal predictors output the meaningless conditional mean at stochastic branching transitions. On OGBench offline data with leak-free evaluation, planning over its predicted modes reaches up to 0.85 success versus 0.02 to 0.09 for single-predictor rollouts, beating deterministic, gated-MoE, and variational baselines on every task.

title: Prompting Claude Fable 5
blurb: Anthropic published a prompting guide for Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5, covering the scaffolding changes needed to move from Claude Opus 4.8, which include adaptive-only thinking, summarized thinking output, and a new refusal stop reason. Fable 5 targets long-running, ambiguous tasks that take hours to weeks, and runs safety classifiers on offensive cybersecurity and life sciences work that can be routed to Opus 4.8 via fallback.

title: GLM 5.2 and the coming AI margin collapse
blurb: GLM 5.2 from Z.ai is the first open-weights model the author considers a genuine competitor to Opus and GPT-5.5, hard to distinguish from his daily driver in practice. The argument is that frontier labs run roughly 90% gross margins on inference, and a comparable open model that anyone can host collapses that spread. Training is a fixed cost, but inference is where the profit lives, and open weights put it under pressure.

title: European banking watchdogs ECB and ESRB warn that frontier AI models pose "systemic risks to the financial system", giving lenders four months to prepare (Financial Times)
blurb: The ECB and ESRB warned that frontier AI models pose systemic risks to the financial system, giving lenders four months to prepare for the exposures they create. The European watchdogs flagged concentration risk from banks relying on a small number of AI providers, alongside the potential for correlated failures and herd behavior across automated trading and lending decisions.

title: CompactionRL: Reinforcement Learning with Context Compaction for Long-Horizon Agents
blurb: CompactionRL is a reinforcement learning method that trains long-horizon agentic LLMs to summarize prior interaction states and continue under a compressed context, jointly optimizing task execution and summary generation. Applied to open models, it lifts GLM-4.5-Air (106B-A30B) to 66.8% Pass@1 on SWE-bench Verified and 24.5% on Terminal-Bench 2.0, gains of 7.0 and 3.1 points.

title: Anthropic Finds Claude Built an Internal Workspace That Mirrors Human Thought
blurb: Anthropic published a 16-author paper describing the "J-space," a small internal workspace holding a few dozen concepts that Claude reasons with and that emerged during training rather than being designed in. A companion tool called the Jacobian lens reads concepts the model never outputs, surfacing "blackmail" and "manipulation" in safety tests before Claude acted, and Google DeepMind's Neel Nanda independently reproduced the core findings on the open-weight Qwen 3.6 27B.

title: Untrusted Content Masking for Web Agents with Security Guarantees
blurb: This paper introduces Untrusted Content Masking (UCM), a defense that restores the trust boundary web agents need to guarantee protection against prompt injection attacks. UCM uses a webpage's DOM to distinguish trusted from untrusted regions without reading their content, redacting untrusted regions before they reach the agent and routing interaction through a sandboxed interface with strict privilege separation.

title: Agent Data Injection Attacks are Realistic Threats to AI Agents
blurb: This paper introduces agent data injection (ADI), a new class of indirect prompt injection where attackers disguise malicious payloads as trusted data like resource identifiers or tool call formats, causing agents to execute unintended actions while bypassing existing defenses. The authors found critical vulnerabilities enabling arbitrary click attacks on web agents including Claude in Chrome and Nanobrowser, plus remote code execution and supply-chain attacks on Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI.

title: [AINews] The Field Guide to Fable
blurb: This talk lays out how to work effectively with Fable-class models, centered on "unhobbling": the idea that most limits on a model come from the harnesses and prompts we wrap it in, so encountering a stronger model should prompt you to strip those constraints away. It covers techniques for surfacing your own blind spots, such as asking Claude for a "blindspot pass" or keeping a running log of underspecified decisions, and argues that greater capability means refusing the usual good-fast-cheap tradeoffs.

title: Nvidia pushes Kyber release to 2028 following manufacturing concerns: report
blurb: Nvidia delayed its Kyber rack-scale architecture by more than a year to 2028 after hitting manufacturing problems with the PCB midplane that connects the modules, SemiAnalysis reported. The setback also threatens the NVL576 system and leaves Nvidia without a proven way to scale up Rubin Ultra, following last week's report that it canceled the four-die Rubin Ultra design.

title: LeRobot v0.6.0: Imagine, Evaluate, Improve
blurb: LeRobot v0.6.0 adds world model policies (VLA-JEPA, LingBot-VA, FastWAM) that learn to imagine future frames during training, a batch of new VLAs, and a reward models API for detecting task success. The release unifies six simulation benchmarks under lerobot-eval, adds a lerobot-rollout CLI with DAgger-style human corrections, FSDP and cloud training on HF Jobs, and up to 2x faster data loading.

title: TeraWulf signs 20-year data center lease with Anthropic expected to generate $19 billion in revenue
blurb: TeraWulf signed a 20-year lease with Anthropic for a purpose-built AI infrastructure campus in Hawesville, Kentucky, expected to generate roughly $19 billion in revenue. The site will deliver 401 MW of critical IT load in phases starting in the second half of 2027, part of a broader shift by bitcoin miners into AI compute to secure predictable revenue.

title: Let Fable use its own judgement rather than dictating how it should work
blurb: The author argues that Fable produces better results when told to use its own judgement rather than given rigid rules for how to work. The example is testing: instead of specifying when to write automated tests, just let the model decide. He extends this to cost control, prompting Fable to delegate smaller coding tasks to lower-power models in subagents while keeping judgment and review on the main loop, which noticeably slowed how fast his token allowance drained.

title: OpenRouter: Chinese AI models have drawn 30%+ of token use by US companies each week since February 8, peaking at 46%, up from 11% over the previous 12 months (Kai Nicol-Schwarz/CNBC)
blurb: Chinese AI models have accounted for more than 30% of weekly token usage by US companies since February 8, according to OpenRouter data cited by CNBC, with a peak of 46%. That share is up sharply from 11% over the prior 12 months, signaling faster enterprise adoption of open models from labs like DeepSeek and Alibaba despite ongoing US-China tensions.

title: TeraWulf Signs $19 Billion Lease With Anthropic for AI-Infrastructure Campus
blurb: TeraWulf signed a $19B lease with Anthropic to build an AI-infrastructure campus in Kentucky. The deal gives Anthropic dedicated data-center capacity as it races to secure compute, with the agreement projected to generate roughly $19 billion in revenue for TeraWulf over its term.

title: China's Alibaba bans Anthropic AI for employees after 'distillation attack' accusation
blurb: Alibaba will ban employees from using Anthropic's Claude tools for work starting July 10, placing Claude Code on a high-risk software list and requiring staff to switch to its in-house assistant Qoder. The move follows Anthropic's June letter to a US Senate committee accusing Alibaba of carrying out "the largest known distillation attack" on its models.

title: OpenAI can generate ChatGPT Ads for you
blurb: OpenAI added a feature to ChatGPT Ads that generates ad variations for advertisers based on their website and campaign settings. Marketers select "Add new ad," let ChatGPT draft an ad, then review, edit, and approve it before it goes live on the ChatGPT Ad platform.

title: Microsoft wants to keep your AI agents from going rogue
blurb: Microsoft introduced Microsoft Execution Containers (MXC), a policy-driven execution layer for AI agents on Windows and WSL, now in early preview. Developers define constraints that Windows enforces at runtime, with the initial release supporting process isolation, already used by GitHub Copilot CLI, and session isolation that separates agents from the user's desktop, clipboard, and active sessions.

title: Meta rebuilds its AI storage stack from the ground up to stop GPUs sitting idle
blurb: Meta rebuilt the BLOB storage layer atop its Tectonic fabric to keep AI training data from bottlenecking idle GPUs, cutting cross-region data movement time by up to 97%. The redesign collapsed fragmented metadata into a single ZippyDB-backed schema for near-instant path lookups, replaced the dataplane proxy with a fat-client architecture that streams bytes directly from storage servers, and colocated storage regionally with the GPUs that need it.

title: MIRAGE: Defending Long-Form RAG Against Misinformation Pollution
blurb: MIRAGE is a training-free, model-agnostic defense that hardens long-form RAG against polluted retrievals containing subtle misinformation, misleading framings, or fabrications. It builds an NLI-based cross-document claim graph and uses a Defended-Claims Gate to either ground generation in a multi-source supported subset or block retrieval and answer parametrically, restoring factuality under mixed and fully polluted evidence across four QA benchmarks.

title: Privacy-Preserving Robustness Verification for Neural Networks
blurb: SecureCROWN is the first framework for privacy-preserving neural network robustness verification, using secure two-party computation to let a model owner and data owner jointly compute certified robustness bounds while revealing only the final result. It eliminates the data-dependent branching in Linear Bound Propagation by reformulating conditional logic as continuous arithmetic, matching plaintext verification results in 0.1 to 200 seconds across LAN and WAN settings.

title: MetaSkill-Evolve: Recursive Self-Improvement of LLM Agents via Two-Timescale Meta-Skill Evolution
blurb: MetaSkill-Evolve is a two-timescale framework that makes LLM agent skill improvement recursive, evolving both the task skill an agent uses and the meta-skill that governs how it improves. Task skills update on a fast loop while a five-component meta-skill parameterizing the Analyzer, Retriever, Allocator, Proposer, and Evolver agents evolves on a slower one, lifting held-out accuracy over the raw backbone by 23.54, 16.09, and 1.92 points on OfficeQA, SealQA, and ALFWorld.

title: Is Three the Magic Number? An Empirical Evaluation of LLM-Based Repair Loops
blurb: This paper evaluates how many iterations LLM-based repair loops actually need across code generation, test generation, and code translation, finding a consistent pattern of diminishing returns where the first three to four iterations capture most of the gains. The authors also show that workflow orchestration and feedback design shape repair behavior more than the underlying model, and argue repair budgets should be treated as an explicit experimental variable.

title: Train Smarter, Not Longer: Memorization-Guided Data Reuse for Efficient LLM Training
blurb: This paper proposes Memorization-guided Data Reuse, a multi-epoch training method that uses "Memorization Window" signals from loss retention dynamics and downstream scores to decide adaptively when and how to replay limited high-quality data. Preliminary experiments show performance keeps improving with repetition well past the commonly cited four-epoch limit, though the authors leave a full scheduler to future work.

title: Pruning RAG context down to what the answer actually needs
blurb: Kapa added a small, cheap LLM between its RAG retriever and generator that reads the question and all retrieved chunks together, then drops the chunks the answer won't need before the expensive model sees them. The pruner cuts about 68% of context while keeping roughly 96% of recall, lowering per-query cost by a third net of its own cost.

title: AMD Ryzen AI Halo – $4k AI Dev Kit
blurb: AMD released the Ryzen AI Halo, a $3,999 mini-PC built around the 16-core Ryzen AI Max+ 395 with Radeon 8060S integrated graphics, an NPU, and 128 GB of unified LPDDR5x-8000 memory at 256 GB/s. The 6-inch box ships with a removable 2 TB SSD and a custom AMD Debian 13.4 Linux image tuned for ROCm-based local AI development.

title: Google Is Suing Chinese Scammers Who Are Using Gemini
blurb: Google filed suit against Outsider Enterprise, a China-based group that sold phishing-as-a-service through Telegram and instructed customers on using Gemini to build fake Google, YouTube, and government sites. The operation offered nearly 300 scam templates, and Google worked with AT&T, Verizon, and T-Mobile to block the malicious texts, while its on-device Google Messages detection blocks roughly 10 billion scam texts monthly.

title: Tencent's Apache-licensed Hy3 takes on GLM-5.2 at half the size — and wins everywhere except coding
blurb: Tencent's Hunyuan team released the full version of Hy3, a 295B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model with 21B active parameters, under the permissive Apache 2.0 license. The model matches or beats the roughly twice-larger GLM-5.2 across most benchmarks except coding, and Tencent is offering it free on OpenRouter for two weeks.

title: DSpark: Confidence-Scheduled Speculative Decoding with Semi-Autoregressive Generation
blurb: DSpark is a speculative decoding framework that pairs a semi-autoregressive drafter, which adds a lightweight sequential module to a parallel backbone to model intra-block dependencies, with confidence-scheduled verification that tunes each request's verification length by estimated prefix survival probability. Deployed in DeepSeek-V4's serving system under live traffic, it accelerated per-user generation by 60% to 85% over the MTP-1 production baseline at matched throughput.

title: Anthropic inks $19B AI data center lease with TeraWulf
blurb: Anthropic signed a 20-year, $19B lease for a TeraWulf data center under construction in Hawesville, Kentucky, that will deliver 401 megawatts of computing power once fully operational in 2028. TeraWulf plans to invest $3B to $4B in the campus, less than a fifth of the lease's value, and expects to bring initial capacity online in the second half of 2027.

title: Streaming Neural Speech Codecs through Time-Invariant Representations
blurb: TiCodec is a neural speech codec that factorizes audio into time-varying content and time-invariant speaker and environment information via a Time-Invariant Representation Extraction module. This paper introduces Dual-TIRE, a multi-level architecture that combines complementary encoder layers to improve reconstruction quality and speaker similarity, and shows the codec runs in a streaming setting with 660ms processing blocks without significant quality loss.

title: Small AI Models Gain Traction In places with unreliable networks
blurb: Small AI models that run locally on phones and low-power devices are gaining traction in regions with unreliable networks and electricity. RxScanner, a handheld spectrometer that authenticates medication by its molecular profile, shifted to an on-device model after a US data center 14,000km away took over five minutes per scan. The World Bank notes only 0.7% of internet users in the poorest countries have used ChatGPT, versus a quarter in developed nations.

title: Latent Programming Horizons in Coding Agents
blurb: This paper shows that the residual streams of language models driving coding agents linearly encode properties of the evolving program, with logistic-regression probes decoding whether code parses, passes tests, or introduces regressions at AUC up to 0.83 across two models and benchmarks. More surprisingly, probes predict the outcomes of future edits before they are written to disk, reaching above-chance accuracy roughly 25 steps ahead in what the authors call the agent's latent programming horizon.

title: Can LLMs Really Recover Microservice Failures? A Recovery-Aware Evaluation of Diagnosis-to-Action Reasoning
blurb: R2Act is a recovery-action evaluation framework for post-diagnosis incident response, instantiated as a benchmark of 302 quality-audited Kubernetes incidents with synchronized multi-modal observations, root-cause labels, and annotated valid and invalid recovery plans. The strongest RAG-based LLMs reach 91.4% to 99.7% root-cause accuracy but only 36.8% to 60.3% recovery validity, showing that failures stem from translating diagnoses into valid actions rather than missing diagnostic knowledge.

title: US cyber agency is using Anthropic’s Mythos to audit government code, sources say
blurb: CISA is reportedly using Anthropic's Mythos model to scan government code repositories for vulnerabilities that could expose federal systems to foreign spies or cybercriminals, according to three people who spoke to Reuters. The work, run by the agency's Attack Surface Evaluation team, has already surfaced a large number of bugs, though neither Anthropic nor CISA has confirmed operational details on the record.

title: Secret Claude tracker shocks users after Anthropic’s anti-surveillance stance
blurb: Anthropic removed hidden tracking code from Claude Code after security researcher "Thereallo" exposed that it used "prompt steganography" to covertly flag Chinese users' timezone, proxy, and possible ties to AI labs accused of distillation. Engineer Thariq Shihipar confirmed the March "experiment" was meant to curb reseller abuse and distillation, but privacy advocates called it a breach of trust from a company that recently sued the White House over user surveillance.

title: Graph Sparse Sampling: Breaking the Curse of the Horizon in Continuous MDP Planning
blurb: Graph Sparse Sampling (GSS) is an online planning algorithm for continuous MDPs that shares sampled futures across candidate decisions instead of branching separate successors per action, producing a branch-free graph that exposes large GPU-friendly batches. The authors prove finite-sample bounds with polynomial rather than exponential dependence on the planning horizon, and show GSS outperforming tree-based planners like MCTS on long-horizon continuous-control tasks.

title: Import AI 464: Fable writes GPU kernels; AI automation; and analog computation
blurb: Fable wrote the fastest megakernel ever submitted to KernelBench-Mega, hitting an 18.71X speedup over an optimized PyTorch baseline versus 14.4X for Claude Opus 4.8 and 4.34X for GPT 5.5. Its solution used exactly one cooperative kernel launch per decoded token, while every other top entry needed 4 to 14. The author argues kernel design is a core input to AI R&D, making the benchmark a signal on progress toward recursive self-improvement.

title: Noisy-Channel Minimum Bayes Risk Decoding
blurb: This paper introduces a noisy-channel decomposition of Minimum Bayes Risk decoding that splits it into four components: hypothesis-to-reference likelihood, reference-to-hypothesis likelihood, hypothesis prior, and reference prior. The decomposition accounts for the asymmetry of metrics like BLEU and COMET, unifies existing MBR variants, and suggests that task-specific channel weighting can outperform standard MBR decoding.

title: Rating the Pitch, Not the Product: User Evaluations of LLMs Reflect Expectations More Than Performance
blurb: A controlled study of 162 participants found that user ratings of LLMs track pre-interaction framing, not actual performance. Participants who saw a landing page overstating a model's capability rated it more favorably and used more directive prompting, while output quality depended only on the model's true capability. Post-use impressions were predicted by whether the model met expectations and user confidence, not task performance, suggesting preference data behind public leaderboards measures expectation management as much as the model itself.

title: The AI Superforecasters Are Here
blurb: The author argues that AI superforecasters have arrived, pointing to founders whose scaffolded frontier models turned $35 into $2 million on Kalshi over seven months and beat Kalshi, Polymarket, and the stock market by roughly 25%. These systems wrap models like Claude or ChatGPT in a research scaffold that deploys subagents and reads dozens of sources per question, matching earlier forecasts that AI would surpass top human forecasters around 2026-2027.

title: Galaxy delivers 133 MW of critical IT load to CoreWeave as Helios bitcoin mine turns AI hub
blurb: Galaxy Digital delivered 133 MW of critical IT load to CoreWeave under a 15-year lease, completing Phase I of its Helios campus in West Texas and its conversion from bitcoin mining to AI infrastructure. CoreWeave, itself a former Ethereum miner, has committed to 526 MW across three phases at the site, expected to generate more than $1B in average annual revenue.

title: MPs tell Brit government: Sort out your tech sovereignty or get left out in the cold
blurb: A UK parliamentary committee warned the government that failing to build sovereign AI and compute capacity risks leaving Britain dependent on foreign providers and sidelined in the global tech race. The MPs called for stronger domestic infrastructure investment and clearer strategy, arguing that continued reliance on overseas hardware and cloud platforms undermines national security and economic competitiveness.

title: OptiAgent: End-to-End Optimization Modeling via Multi-Agent Iterative Refinement
blurb: OptiAgent is a multi-agent framework that turns a natural language description of an Operations Research problem into a solver-ready mathematical formulation and executable code. It prioritizes the modeling step with dedicated agents that extract decision variables and constraints, and a multi-loop validation architecture with four feedback mechanisms targeting distinct failure modes, reaching state-of-the-art results on 3 of 4 LP, MILP, and Nonlinear Programming benchmarks.

title: Madlad builds homebrew GPU using 8,192 RISC-V chips
blurb: A developer built a homebrew GPU from 8,192 RISC-V cores, wiring the chips together into a massively parallel accelerator for graphics and compute workloads. The project demonstrates that open instruction-set hardware can be scaled into GPU-class parallelism outside the major vendors, though it trades raw performance and power efficiency for the transparency and low cost of commodity RISC-V silicon.

title: Survey: Chinese companies plan to allocate 46% of their AI accelerator budget to domestic products in the next 12 months, up from 30% today, a shift from Nvidia (Gao Yuan/Bloomberg)
blurb: A Bernstein survey found Chinese companies plan to direct 46% of their AI accelerator budgets to domestic chips over the next 12 months, up from 30% today. The shift marks a move away from Nvidia as buyers turn to local suppliers like Huawei and Cambricon amid US export restrictions and Beijing's push for self-sufficiency.

title: Why sandboxing your agent is not enough
blurb: This CNCF post compares two Kubernetes-native projects for running AI agents securely: agent-sandbox, a SIG Apps CRD and controller that gives agents strong identities, persistent storage, and isolated pod lifecycles, and agent-substrate, a standalone runtime aimed at higher scale and efficiency. Agent-substrate runs agents in short bursts on secure worker pods that suspend when idle and resume on any available worker, decoupling worker lifecycle from the agent actor.

title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
blurb: Sysdig documented JadePuffer, which it called the first "agentic ransomware" operation, in which an AI agent handled the full technical execution of an attack, breaking into a Langflow server, moving to a production MySQL database, encrypting over 1,300 configuration records, and writing its own ransom note. A human still provisioned the infrastructure, chose the victim, and supplied the stolen credentials, so the agent ran the intrusion but did not operate without oversight.

title: Chinese Firms Leave Nvidia for Local AI Suppliers, Survey Shows
blurb: Chinese firms are abandoning Nvidia's advanced accelerators in favor of domestic silicon, according to a new survey on shifting AI infrastructure spending. The trend reflects escalating US-China tensions and Beijing's push to substitute American technology, accelerating a broader realignment of the country's AI hardware supply chain toward local suppliers.

title: Chinese AI models are gaining ground with U.S. companies as OpenAI, Anthropic costs surge
blurb: Chinese-built AI models are gaining traction with U.S. companies, which routed over 30% of their OpenRouter tokens through models from DeepSeek, Z.ai, and other Chinese labs each week since Feb. 8, peaking at 46%. The shift is driven by cost as token prices rise at OpenAI and Anthropic, with AI startup Lindy moving 100% of its traffic from Claude to DeepSeek in June.

title: By Year-End, Anthropic Will Out-Earn Every Public Software Company Except Microsoft
blurb: Anthropic exited 2025 at roughly $9B in run-rate revenue and hit $47B by mid-May 2026, already larger than Salesforce, Adobe, Intuit, ServiceNow, and Workday. On its current trajectory the run-rate is tracking toward $70B to $90B by December, which would leave Microsoft as the only public software company still out-earning the three-year-old startup.

title: Cortex: A Bidirectionally Aligned Embodied Agent Framework for Long-horizon Manipulation
blurb: Cortex is a bidirectionally aligned embodied agent framework for long-horizon manipulation that bridges the gap between high-level VLM planning and low-level VLA execution using 32 canonical skill primitives. It outperforms monolithic baselines by 3.1% on Libero-long and 4.1% on RoboTwin, and enables zero-shot completion of unseen real-world tasks like multi-stage chemistry experiments by combining a generalist VLM with a fine-tuned VLA.

title: Adaptive Inference Batching using Policy Gradients
blurb: This paper trains REINFORCE and PPO agents to learn adaptive inference batching and routing policies, testing them against static heuristics on a discrete-event simulator validated with Azure Functions and BurstGPT traces. RL offered only marginal gains for single-GPU Poisson traffic but delivered a 3.5x improvement over Round-Robin and 48% over Shortest-Queue in multi-GPU heterogeneous routing by discovering a workload-segregation policy that eliminates Head-of-Line blocking.

title: Multiplayer Interactive World Models with Representation Autoencoders
blurb: This paper introduces the first multiplayer world model, a 5-billion-parameter latent diffusion system trained on 10,000 hours of Rocket League gameplay that conditions on multiple agents' action streams to attribute scene changes to the correct player. It generates four-player matches in real time at 20 frames per second on a single Nvidia B200 GPU, stays stable for hours despite training only on short clips, and ships with a released dataset, training codebase, and live demo.

title: Is the Geometry Doing the Work? An Operating-Point Audit of Hierarchy in Hyperbolic Vision-Language Models
blurb: This paper audits three hyperbolic vision-language models, MERU, HyCoCLIP, and PHyCLIP, and finds that none actually use their curved geometry, with every converged checkpoint sitting near-Euclidean and the entailment cone and traversal machinery measured inoperative. The authors trace the collapse to a low-curvature, wide-cone shortcut in the entailment objective, and distill their diagnostics into a five-number geometry report for evaluating future hierarchy claims.

title: ToolFailBench: Diagnosing Tool-Use Failures in LLM Agents
blurb: ToolFailBench is a diagnostic benchmark of 1,000 tasks across finance, medicine, law, cybersecurity, and real estate that labels agent traces as Tool-Skip, Result-Ignore, Output-Fabrication, or Unnecessary-Tool-Use rather than reporting only final accuracy. Across 19 models the best reaches an 86.33% Clean Tool-Use Rate, and models with similar aggregate scores fail differently, with Llama-3.1-70B and Qwen2.5-72B differing by 89 percentage points on control-task accuracy.

title: Retroactive Chain-of-Thought (RetroCoT): Forensic Reconstruction Prompts as a Safety Diagnostic Across Model Generations
blurb: This paper introduces Retroactive Chain-of-Thought (RetroCoT), a single-turn attack that reframes harmful requests as forensic reconstruction tasks and achieves a 58% attack success rate on gpt-4o versus 0% for direct requests. GPT-5 models refuse it outright, but one adversarial feedback turn continuing the forensic frame raises success back to 94% on gpt-4o and 48% on GPT-5.4-mini.

title: Rebuilding Cognition's Agentic MapReduce
blurb: Cognition's Security Swarm uses a technique called Agentic MapReduce to find vulnerabilities in large codebases, sharding agents across a threat model and finding more bugs at roughly 30% lower cost than other agentic solutions. This post reverse-engineers that loop into a task-agnostic version for research, coding, and review, using git branches for agent-to-agent communication and git merge to power the reduce step.

title: The context engineering playbook
blurb: nao co-founder and CEO Claire Gouze shares a practical playbook for building a reliable context layer that agents can query in natural language. She argues context engineering is the new analytics engineering: the job of turning tacit business knowledge into something structured and trustworthy hasn't changed, only the medium, markdown and files instead of data models. The playbook includes specific guidance on building your own context layer and creating evals.

title: Amazon Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base
blurb: AWS released Amazon Bedrock Managed Knowledge Base, which abstracts storage, retrieval, embeddings, re-ranking, and foundation model selection into a single managed primitive for building RAG applications on proprietary data. The service auto-selects default embedding, re-ranker, and foundation models, and ships six pre-built connectors that pull enterprise data and permissions directly from SaaS applications.

title: PRX Part 4: Our Data Strategy
blurb: Photoroom detailed its data strategy for training PRX, a 7B image generation model, assembling pre-training data from a mix of public and internal datasets and re-captioning images with a VLM into a streamable corpus. The team prioritized breadth and diversity over aesthetic filtering, using long, faithful captions and building on existing quality-filtered sources rather than curating from scratch, leaving polish to fine-tuning and preference alignment.

title: Contextual Policies in Omnigent: Using session state to better govern AI agents
blurb: Databricks introduced contextual policies in Omnigent, its open-source meta-harness that governs AI agents based on what a session has already done rather than evaluating each tool call in isolation. Because the policies track session state like accumulated spend or whether untrusted content was read, they support per-session budget caps and guardrails that tighten as risk builds, and apply across wrapped agents including Claude Code and Codex.

title: Paris-based UMA, founded by ex-Tesla Optimus scientist Rémi Cadene and ex-Google DeepMind researcher Pierre Sermanet, demos its Northstar AI humanoid robot (Benoit Berthelot/Bloomberg)
blurb: UMA, a Paris-based robotics startup founded by ex-Tesla Optimus scientist Rémi Cadene and ex-Google DeepMind researcher Pierre Sermanet, demonstrated its Northstar AI humanoid robot. The company is positioning its founders' backgrounds in embodied AI and robot learning as the basis for building a European competitor to US and Chinese humanoid programs.

title: AI Legal Startup Norm Valued at $1.2 Billion in Funding Round
blurb: Norm Ai raised $120 million at a $1.2 billion valuation to automate legal services and rethink how the industry bills for its work. The funding backs Norm's push to shift legal work away from hourly billing toward AI-driven compliance and regulatory automation.

title: Nvidia-Backed Startup Nscale Locks in $900 Million for Data-Center Buildout
blurb: Nscale, the U.K.-based AI infrastructure startup backed by Nvidia, raised $900M to accelerate its data-center buildout across Europe, the U.S., and Asia Pacific. The company said the funds would provide flexible liquidity to expand capacity as demand for AI compute grows in each region.

title: Advances in Neural Controlled Differential Equations
blurb: This thesis advances Neural Controlled Differential Equations, continuous-time models that solve a dynamical system driven by an input path to handle irregularly sampled time series, through three methods: Log-NCDEs, Linear NCDEs, and Structured Linear NCDEs. Together they cut the time per training step by up to three orders of magnitude while reaching state-of-the-art performance across diverse time series benchmarks.

title: Unified Audio Intelligence Without Regressing on Text Intelligence
blurb: NVIDIA introduced Audex (Nemotron-Labs-Audex-30B-A3B), a unified audio-text MoE LLM that projects encoded audio into the text embedding space and treats text and quantized audio output tokens uniformly in a single Transformer decoder. Trained on 157.4B audio and 320.5B text tokens, it reaches state-of-the-art results on audio understanding, speech recognition, translation, TTS, and speech-to-speech while showing marginal or no regression on its text backbone's reasoning and agentic capabilities.

title: Localized LoRA-MoE: Block-wise Low-Rank Experts With Adaptive Routing
blurb: Localized LoRA-MoE combines block-wise low-rank experts with context-conditioned routing to keep multi-task fine-tuning from collapsing adapter weights into unspecialized averages. The paper tests two variants, centralized macro-routing across the full grid and decentralized per-cell gating, and finds that cell-level gating matches a global coordinator while isolating faults so sensor failures or corrupted pathways don't propagate.

title: RustMizan: A Compilable, Contamination-Aware Benchmarking Framework for Rust Vulnerabilities
blurb: RustMizan is a benchmarking framework for Rust vulnerability analysis with compilable code variants at the crate, file, and function levels, annotated for binary detection, CWE classification, and line-level localization. Across four frontier models in an agentic setup, binary classification lands in the 56-65% range while line localization F1 stays near 20%, and adversarial cues drop line F1 by about 27%.

title: From Multiplicity to Vulnerability: Privacy Amplification Risk from One-Dataset-Multiple-Model Exposure
blurb: This paper shows that reusing one dataset to train multiple task-specific models, each deployed as a separate API, substantially amplifies privacy leakage, a phenomenon the authors term ODMM privacy composition. Their PRIME attack aggregates black-box membership inference signals across the exposed models and consistently outperforms single-task attacks across five benchmarks and architectures from ResNet and ViT to Qwen3-1.7B, even under differential privacy.

title: AMRM-Pure: Semantic-Preserving Adversarial Purification
blurb: This paper introduces AMRM-Pure, an adversarial purification framework that removes perturbations by preserving semantic relationships between image patches rather than closing the feature-space gap with diffusion generators. The authors show that Attentive Mask Reconstruction Models are highly sensitive to adversarial noise, and formulate denoising as a tractable input optimization applied to MAE and MaskDiT architectures, setting new state-of-the-art results across multiple benchmarks.

title: A Comprehensive Study of Implementation Bugs in Multi-modal Agents
blurb: This paper presents the first systematic study of implementation bugs specific to multi-modal agents, analyzing 158 M-agent bugs drawn from 1,268 issue reports across 34 agents and organizing them into a taxonomy by symptom and root cause. The authors also built MATester, a proof-of-concept bug identifier that covered 61.4% of known open issues and surfaced 31 new bugs across 12 additional agents.

title: Nvidia taps AI cloud providers to expand compute access for startups
blurb: Nvidia launched a partnership program that routes AI startups to its network of cloud providers for full-stack compute, with participating firms sharing product and cloud revenue back to Nvidia. Two initial Australian providers anchor the scheme: Sharon AI will deploy up to 40,000 GPUs, while Firmus Technologies is building a 360-megawatt data center in Batam, Indonesia, slated to house up to 170,000 Nvidia GPUs.

title: Samsung says memory chip demand will drive 19-fold jump in operating profit
blurb: Samsung projected a 19-fold jump in second-quarter operating profit to a record 89.4 trillion won (around $58.47 billion), driven by record memory chip prices and demand from AI servers. Quarterly revenue is expected to top 171 trillion won, more than double a year earlier, easing analyst concerns about the sustainability of AI-linked spending.

title: Zscaler finds autonomous agents succumb to IPI traps
blurb: Zscaler tested 26 LLMs against indirect prompt injection (IPI) traps and found several autonomous agents fell for scams that few humans would, with susceptibility varying by model rather than by price. Four models, including Gemini-2.5-pro and Llama3-3-70b-instruct, failed to take appropriate actions, while some cheaper models resisted better. Critics counter that a binary safe/vulnerable label is too simplistic, since an agent's behavior shifts as it ingests new data and a failed test can pass an hour later.

title: Researchers make the case for a cybersecurity AI scientist
blurb: Researchers at the Chinese Academy of Sciences argue that autonomous AI is transforming security operations faster than security research itself, and propose a Cybersecurity AI Scientist that runs the full loop from question to experimental design, tool building, controlled execution, and written result. Their prototype, Hephaestus, is a modular multi-agent system with role-specialized agents for problem framing, threat modeling, tool generation, and reporting, built to produce both offensive and defensive work.

title: From Failing to Passing: Evolving Natural Language Prompt Optimization Rules for LLM Code Generation
blurb: This paper introduces DUALFIX, a staged repair pipeline that pairs search-evolved natural language prompt transformation rules with execution-feedback repair to fix LLM code generation failures at both the specification and implementation level. The evolved rules are error-agnostic and transfer zero-shot across models, recovering up to 30% of baseline failures and fixing 3-5 times more failing cases than Self-Fix on LiveCodeBench and APPS.

title: Look-Ahead-Freedom as Temporal Non-Interference: A Verifiable Correctness Property for Backtesting and Agentic Trading Pipelines
blurb: This paper reframes look-ahead bias, where a backtest uses information from after a decision epoch, as temporal non-interference over a time-indexed information lattice. The authors prove the general case is undecidable, but give a sound, linear-time type-and-effect system for the value-independent fragment covering windowing, resampling, joins, and agentic retrieval that catches every planted leak differential and tiling detectors miss.

title: OfficeCLI: Office suite for AI agents to read and edit Microsoft Office files
blurb: OfficeCLI is an open-source, single-binary Office suite that gives AI agents full read and edit control over Word, Excel, and PowerPoint files without an Office installation or dependencies. Its built-in HTML rendering engine converts .docx, .xlsx, and .pptx to HTML or PNG, closing the render-look-fix loop and installing a skill into agents like Claude Code, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot.

title: Show HN: Pulpie – Models for Cleaning the Web
blurb: Pulpie is an open-source family of encoder models that extract main content from HTML by labeling every block as content or boilerplate in a single forward pass. Its smallest model, pulpie-orange-small, matches the leading extractor Dripper on WebMainBench ROUGE-5 F1 (0.862 vs 0.864) at a third the size and roughly 20x lower cost, processing 13.7 pages/sec versus 0.68 on an NVIDIA L4.

title: Price per 1M tokens is meaningless
blurb: The author argues that comparing models by price per 1M tokens is misleading, since each lab uses its own proprietary tokenizer that splits the same text into different token counts. Anthropic recently modified its tokenizer, causing Claude to split identical text into 30% more tokens, equivalent to a steep price hike. Reasoning length compounds the problem: GPT-5.5 is nominally pricier than Claude Opus 4.8 but completes the Artificial Analysis benchmark at nearly half the cost per task.

title: UK-based AI infrastructure startup Nscale secures a $900M line of credit to expand its data center buildout across Europe, the US, and the Asia Pacific (Mauro Orru/Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Nscale, a UK-based AI infrastructure startup, secured a $900M line of credit to expand its data center buildout across Europe, the US, and the Asia Pacific. The financing will fund additional compute capacity as Nscale grows its footprint to meet rising demand for AI training and inference workloads.

title: Real-World Perturbation Testing of Autonomous Driving Systems
blurb: This paper evaluates 72 camera and LiDAR perturbations across three testing modalities: offline model-level analysis, hardware-in-the-loop execution, and closed-loop testing on a full-scale autonomous vehicle. The authors find that model-level metrics alone fail to identify the most harmful perturbations, as camera perturbations with limited offline impact can still cause unstable control and real-world driving failures.

title: OpenAI, Anthropic, and other top AI labs are offering startups token credits and special promotions, as the AI labs seek lasting streams of enterprise revenue (Wall Street Journal)
blurb: OpenAI, Anthropic, and other top AI labs are offering startups token credits and special promotions to lock in early adoption. The push reflects the labs' shift toward securing durable enterprise revenue as companies scale up their model usage and become harder to displace.

title: Samsung’s Q2 profit jumps 19-fold to 89 trillion won on AI memory demand
blurb: Samsung Electronics guided to second-quarter operating profit of about 89.4 trillion won ($58.4bn), roughly 19 times its year-earlier figure and, by most counts, the largest quarterly operating profit any technology company has reported. The driver is memory pricing, with contract DRAM up about 44% and NAND flash roughly 53% quarter on quarter as AI server demand outruns what the three big memory makers can supply.

title: SovereignPA-Bench: Evaluating User-Owned Personal Agents under Evolving Intent, Platform Mediation, and Consent Constraints
blurb: SovereignPA-Bench is an executable benchmark that evaluates user-owned personal agents on whether they preserve user sovereignty, scoring task success alongside alignment, privacy, consent, evidence, manipulation resistance, user burden, and auditability. Across 120 stress scenarios spanning 4 model families and 8 policy baselines, full-sovereign scaffolding improved sovereignty scores over ReAct, safety-prompt, and other baselines while cutting privacy leakage, consent violations, and manipulation capture.

title: Selective Disclosure Watermarking for Large Language Models
blurb: This paper introduces Hierarchical Vocabulary Routing (HeRo), a watermarking framework that lets different verifiers decode only the portions of an LLM's embedded metadata that match their access level. The method recursively partitions the vocabulary across hierarchical layers, preserving sampling unbiasedness and text quality while supporting fine-grained access control with high detection accuracy and low latency.

title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
blurb: In an interview after its ShipNYC conference, Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch argues the two killer apps for agents are coding agents and internal company agents, with the latter's main challenge being secure, auditable data access. Vercel now sees 6M deployments a day, half triggered by coding agents, and over 1T tokens flowing through its AI gateway daily. Its Sandbox tool cages agents to control what data can enter and leave.

title: PiSAs: Benchmarking Contextual Integrity in Multi-User Agentic Systems
blurb: PiSAs is a benchmark for measuring unintended privacy leaks in shared multi-user agent systems, using dual contextual-integrity annotations that track whether information is appropriate for a task and which users may legitimately access it. It evaluates cross-user spillage across outputs, inter-agent messages, and shared memory, finding that even state-of-the-art models fail to reliably filter inappropriate content or restrict transmission to authorized users.

title: Governed Individuation: Cryptographically Decoupling an Agent's Learning from Its Authority
blurb: This paper introduces governed individuation, an architecture that binds an agent to a cryptographically frozen identity at boot and routes every action through a gate defined by its semantic effect rather than its name. The authors prove no amount of learning can widen an agent's authority without an operator-signed change, and show the gate drops false-allows from 75% under name-based gating to zero while preserving task success.

title: The New Shape of Search: How Conversational AI Recomposes Information Seeking
blurb: This paper links real conversations with major AI assistants to the same users' searches and browsing to reconstruct full information-seeking episodes. It finds conversational AI reshapes seeking rather than just reducing it: most episodes terminate in place, but roughly a third scaffold into longer multi-step journeys, with collapse driven by how briefly the user phrases the opening ask, not the task type. Search persists through about three-quarters of within-episode transitions, and explicit verification follows only around 1% of episodes.

title: Leanstral
blurb: Mistral released Leanstral, an Apache-2.0 series of generalist code-agent models for Lean 4 with 119B total and 6B active parameters that operate inside the open-source Mistral Vibe harness rather than a bespoke prover scaffold. It saturates miniF2F, solves 587/672 PutnamBench problems, reaches a state-of-the-art 34% on FATE-X, and found previously unknown bugs in real Lean and translated Rust repositories.

title: Clouded Judgement 7.3.26 - The End of Compute Scarcity? Not So Fast
blurb: SpaceX and Meta renting out compute has fueled a bear thesis that the compute crunch is ending, but this analysis argues the signal is misleading. SpaceX's ~$2.32B monthly GPU deals with Anthropic, Google, and Reflection carry 90-day exit clauses and extreme pricing, structured because SpaceX plans to reclaim the capacity itself and buyers had nowhere else to go. Meta, meanwhile, hasn't sold anything yet.

title: Chinese AI Startup DeepSeek Developing Own AI Chip, Report Says
blurb: DeepSeek is developing its own AI chip to power its systems, Reuters reported, citing unnamed sources. The move would reduce the Chinese startup's reliance on NVIDIA hardware amid US export controls, joining domestic efforts by companies like Huawei and Cambricon to build homegrown alternatives.

title: Optimizing ML Workload Partitioning between CPUs and CIM Accelerators for Heterogeneous Computing
blurb: This paper proposes an Integer Linear Programming framework for partitioning ML inference workloads across CPUs and Computing-in-Memory accelerators, minimizing end-to-end latency under RRAM constraints like limited memory, write latency, and endurance. Combining empirical profiling with analytical models, the heterogeneous CPU-CIM approach reached speedups of up to 30.9x over an edge CPU and 7.3x over a high-performance CPU.

title: Nscale closes a $900m revolving credit facility to fund its data-centre build-out
blurb: Nscale closed a $900m revolving credit facility to accelerate its data-centre build-out across the US, Europe, and Asia-Pacific, syndicated across a dozen banks including J.P. Morgan, Goldman Sachs, and Morgan Stanley. The revolver lets the London firm add debt without diluting shareholders, following a $2bn Series C at a $14.6bn valuation in March and a $1.4bn GPU-backed term loan.

title: Accessibility is the first-class interface for AI agents
blurb: The author argues that AI browser agents should navigate the web through the accessibility tree rather than screenshots, since assistive technologies already proved machines can operate interfaces via structured roles, names, and states. In the author's own agent work, switching from screenshot-based to DOM-native execution cut per-action latency from 2-5 seconds to under 500ms and reduced token cost by an order of magnitude. The open gap is execution: the accessibility tree exposes controls but not a contract for the actions themselves.

title: Synopsys pulls back from chip fab software to chase AI design margins
blurb: Synopsys is preparing to discontinue its manufacturing control software, including the Equipment Engineering System and Fault Detection and Classification tools, and redirect those engineers toward its higher-margin AI chip design business. The firm has told more than 10 chipmakers, including Samsung, SK Hynix, and Kioxia, that the tools have reached end of life, moving to maintenance-only support as it builds out the AI design push behind Nvidia's $2B investment and the $35B Ansys acquisition.

title: AWS caps new users in checkmate for Mechanical Turk microtask platform
blurb: AWS will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk, its crowdsourced human-labor marketplace, effective July 30, 2026, while existing customers retain access. The 2005 service, long used to collect and annotate model-training data, appears squeezed by AI agents now handling the microtasks human workers once performed.

title: From Fixed to Free Cameras: Calibration-Free View-Robust Vision-Language-Action Model
blurb: CamVLA is a Vision-Language-Action model that lets robots operate from unseen camera positions without calibration, depth, or known camera extrinsics. It predicts a camera-centric end-effector action plus a 6-DoF hand-eye matrix relating the camera to the robot base, then composes them geometrically into a base-frame action, improving success rates across diverse viewpoints from a single monocular RGB image.

title: An AI agent ran 600 payloads and wrote its own ransom note in the JadePuffer intrusion, but the part Sysdig quietly admits changes what this attack actually proves about autonomous cybercrime
blurb: Sysdig documented JadePuffer, the first known AI-agent-executed ransomware, though a human still handled setup, infrastructure, target selection, and supplied the credentials.

title: Nscale banks $900M from J.P. Morgan and Goldman Sachs: Is AI infrastructure debt the next bubble?
blurb: Nscale closed a $900M revolving credit facility backed by J.P. Morgan, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, Bank of America, and several other global banks. The London-based AI cloud platform will use the capital to build GPU data centers across the US, Europe, and Asia-Pacific, following a $790M debt raise for its Norway campus and a $2B Series C that valued it at $14.6B.

title: EvoAgentBench: Benchmarking Agent Self-Evolution via Ability Transfer
blurb: EvoAgentBench is a benchmark that isolates agent self-evolution by testing whether procedural Abilities, reusable routines for searching, debugging, and verification, transfer across tasks rather than measuring single-episode accuracy or memory retention. Across a 528/267 train/test split spanning web research, algorithmic reasoning, software engineering, and knowledge work, curated Ability content transferred reliably across model families, but no automatic method sustained positive gains in every setting.

title: Search Beyond What Can Be Taught: Evolving the Knowledge Boundary in Agentic Visual Generation
blurb: This paper introduces SearchGen-Bench and SearchGen-20K, a benchmark of 20,839 prompts across twelve failure categories and twenty-two domains that exposes how visual generators fabricate long-tail world knowledge, with frontier open models scoring just 21 to 28 out of 100. The authors propose a teach-then-search co-training framework that learns each generator's evolving knowledge boundary, deciding when to retrieve external context versus rely on internalized training, and show it produces monotonic improvement.

title: TabPack: Efficient Hyperparameter Ensembles for Tabular Deep Learning
blurb: TabPack is an efficient MLP ensemble for tabular deep learning that samples and trains many MLPs with different hyperparameters in parallel during a single run, selecting ensemble members on the fly. Rather than exact values, it only requires hyperparameter ranges, and with default settings it matched extensively tuned prior methods on medium-to-large datasets, in one case running on a MacBook in less time than tuning baselines took on an industry GPU.

title: Fitted Occupancy-Ratio Evaluation without Bellman Completeness
blurb: This paper introduces fitted occupancy-ratio evaluation (FORE), a fixed-point method for off-policy evaluation that estimates the discounted occupancy ratio through an adjoint Bellman recursion, solving a single-level density-ratio objective on one-step-transition data at each iteration. Unlike fitted Q-evaluation, FORE requires only realizability of the occupancy ratio rather than Bellman completeness, and the authors establish finite-sample KL regret bounds plus support for reward-reweighted, occupancy-weighted, and doubly robust value estimation.

title: On the risk of coding before testing: An empirical study on LLM-based test generation workflow
blurb: This paper shows that LLMs generating tests after writing code systematically replicate the code's faults in the tests, producing implementations and test suites that are mutually consistent and mask defects rather than expose them. Generating tests after faulty code dropped fault detection to 14% versus 25% for tests generated independently, and the bias persisted across prompting strategies including chain-of-thought.

title: Dashboard2Code: Evaluating Multimodal Models on Reconstructing Interactive Dashboards
blurb: Dashboard2Code is a new task that requires multimodal models to actively explore an interactive dashboard, integrate feedback from their own clicks and filters, and generate code that reproduces it. The accompanying DashboardMimic benchmark spans 180 verified Plotly+Dash dashboard-code pairs across eight interaction patterns, and experiments show even the strongest models struggle on high-complexity dashboards while a large open-to-closed-source gap remains.

title: Cam2Sim: Neural Scenario Reconstruction for Closed-Loop Autonomous Driving Simulation
blurb: Cam2Sim is a tool that converts real-world driving recordings into playable CARLA simulation scenarios, reconstructing road geometry, ego trajectories, and parked vehicles from camera images and poses. It augments the scenes with Gaussian Splatting to narrow the visual gap between simulator output and real footage, which the authors show improves closed-loop behavioral similarity to real-world reference runs over a simulation-only baseline.

title: AI Agent Pull Requests on GitHub: Frequency, Structure, and Merge Conflict Rates
blurb: This paper presents the first large-scale analysis of concurrent pull requests from AI coding agents, drawing on the AIDev-pop dataset of 33,596 PRs across 2,807 repositories. Replaying real three-way merges on co-active PR pairs, the authors found textual conflicts in 41.7% of cross-agent pairs versus 19.8% of same-agent pairs, with source-code files driving 84.4% of conflicts.

title: Agentic Autonomy Levels
blurb: Addy Osmani proposes a framework of autonomy levels for agentic engineering, arguing the core activity has shifted from prompting to operating. The piece maps the emerging frontier through concepts like software factories, goal-driven loops, and background sessions, offering a vocabulary for how much control developers hand off as agents take on more of the work.

title: AI Giants Are Handing Out Tons of Free Computing Power to Grab Startup Share
blurb: AI companies are giving startups large amounts of free computing credits to win their business and lock in long-term spending. The push reflects a race for durable enterprise revenue, with providers betting that early adoption on their platforms translates into lasting cloud and API commitments as those startups scale.

title: GORIO: GPU-Centered Remote I/O for Graph ANNS over NVMe-oF
blurb: GORIO is a GPU-centered remote I/O system for graph-based approximate nearest neighbor search over NVMe-oF, keeping query evolution, page-miss generation, and resume decisions on the GPU while the CPU serves only as a transport proxy. On a SIFT1M DiskANN-style workload, it runs 1.31x faster than the state-of-the-art remote-I/O path and 3.73x faster than the direct remote page-cache path.

title: Alibaba’s A.I. Is a Hit, but Hard to Turn Into a Moneymaker
blurb: Alibaba's Qwen models have won broad adoption among developers worldwide, but their open-source licensing makes the success difficult to monetize. Because the models can be downloaded, run, and modified freely, Alibaba captures little direct revenue from the developers building on them, leaving cloud services and enterprise deployments as the primary path to returns.

title: AI startup that’s never turned a profit say's it'll totally be around in 2047 to close its $19B lease
blurb: An unprofitable AI startup signed a roughly $19B data center lease that runs through 2047, betting on decades of future demand despite never having turned a profit. The multi-decade commitment locks in long-term infrastructure costs well beyond the company's current revenue base, underscoring how aggressively AI firms are financing compute against expected rather than realized earnings.

title: Alibaba's Qwen models have made it an AI powerhouse, but the company has struggled to turn their global popularity into a profitable business (New York Times)
blurb: Alibaba's Qwen models have become one of the world's most widely used open model families, but the company has struggled to convert that popularity into profit. The New York Times reports that despite Qwen's global adoption, Alibaba has yet to build a sustainable business around the models as it competes against better-funded US rivals.

title: Alibaba 'bans' Anthropic in AI cold war as cybersecurity regulation heats up in China
blurb: Alibaba reportedly banned employees from using Anthropic's Claude models, citing an alleged backdoor in the code, as tensions between US and Chinese AI firms escalate. The move follows Anthropic's accusation that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to run nearly 29 million exchanges with Claude to distill its capabilities, and coincides with China's revamped Cybersecurity Law and its "Qinglang" cleanup campaign targeting unauthorized foreign LLMs.

title: Backed by Anthropic, OpenAI, and Meta insiders, Bespoke Labs raises $40M: can better environments beat bigger models?
blurb: Bespoke Labs raised $40M across seed and Series A rounds, led by Wing VC and 8VC, with angel backing from individual engineers at Anthropic, OpenAI, and Meta. The company builds simulated enterprise environments, including codebases, Slack threads, tickets, and operational logs, where agents train on long-horizon tasks before deployment.

title: Knowledge Knows, Verbalization Tells: Disentangling Latent Directions for Mathematical Solvability in LLMs
blurb: This paper probes LLM hidden states to show that a model's knowledge of whether a math problem is solvable and its verbalization of that judgment are encoded as distinct, linearly decodable directions. The authors find that fabrication stems mainly from shifts in verbalization rather than the underlying knowledge, and that activation steering on these representations improves abstention.

title: London hyperscaler Nscale secures £670m credit facility
blurb: Nscale, a London-based AI hyperscaler, secured a $900M revolving credit facility from a syndicate including J.P. Morgan, Goldman Sachs, and Morgan Stanley to fund data center expansion across the US, Europe, and APAC. The revolving structure lets Nscale draw, repay, and redraw capital as needed, following its record $1.1B Series B last September and subsequent Series C round.

title: TACTIC-KG: Toward Small Agent Teams for Cyber Threat Intelligence Knowledge Graph Construction
blurb: TACTIC-KG is an agentic framework that builds cybersecurity knowledge graphs from unstructured threat intelligence reports by splitting the task across specialized LLM agents for extraction, typing, verification, and curation. Using lightweight 3B-8B models, it outperforms larger monolithic in-context-learning baselines on extraction F1, typing accuracy, and structural graph similarity while cutting deployment cost.

title: Layer-Parallel Inference Reduces Encrypted Nonlinear Depth in Transformers
blurb: This paper introduces Structured Newton Layer Parallelism (SNLP), a method that cuts the sequential nonlinear depth of encrypted Transformer inference under fully homomorphic encryption from L stages to a few solver iterations plus linear corrections. On a 0.5B model, SNLP reduced symbolic bootstraps from 53 to 20, a 2.65x gain, with only 1.2% higher perplexity and lower error amplification than sequential inference across all eight tested models.

title: Finetuning Lightweight LLMs for Control Flow Graph Generation
blurb: This paper fine-tunes lightweight LLMs to generate Control Flow Graphs directly from source code, avoiding the need for complete, compilable code that bytecode and AST-based tools require. Tested on six models including CodeLlama, QwenCoder, and DeepSeekCoder, the approach handles incomplete or erroneous code and generalizes to programming languages absent from the fine-tuning data.

title: A Temporal Reasoning Benchmarking Framework for LRMs via Difficulty-controlled and Dynamic Test Generation
blurb: TRACE is a benchmarking framework that models temporal reasoning as constraint satisfaction problems via Allen's Interval Algebra, giving fine-grained difficulty control and a Trace-Based Verification Oracle that checks reasoning faithfulness rather than just final answers. Evaluating eight LRMs on its 1,200-instance TRACEBench, the authors found a 28% spurious guessing rate in mid-sized models and distinct failure modes ranging from degenerative loops in small models to reasoning explosion in advanced ones.

title: Exploiting Structural Properties for Efficient Constraint-Aware HNSW Hyperparameter Tuning
blurb: This paper introduces CHAT, a constraint-aware framework for tuning HNSW hyperparameters in vector databases by exploiting structural regularities in the configuration space rather than treating it as a black box. By identifying monotonic, unimodal, and separable relationships to prune infeasible configurations before index construction, CHAT achieves up to 45% higher throughput or 11% higher recall and converges up to 44x faster than strong baselines.

title: xAI is now officially known as SpaceXAI
blurb: xAI has officially rebranded as SpaceXAI, five months after Elon Musk merged his AI and space companies under the SpaceX umbrella, which also folded in the X social network acquired in 2025. The combined company, which went public in June at a $2.1 trillion valuation, is pursuing orbital data centers and has filed with the FCC to launch a million satellites for space-based compute.

title: Time-Series LLMs, Explained with t0-alpha
blurb: This post uses t0-alpha, a 102M-parameter open-weight probabilistic forecaster from The Forecasting Company, to explain how time-series foundation models work: patching a numerical sequence, processing the patches with a causal transformer, and emitting quantiles rather than point forecasts. Re-running GIFT-Eval reproduced the model's reported headline numbers exactly, a CRPS of 0.4941 and a MASE of 0.7240, and the piece maps where such models beat classical baselines and where they still fail.

title: Will Someone Finally Blink in the AI Spending War?
blurb: This piece examines whether any major AI player will pull back from record capital spending, with Meta's rumored plan to rent out excess compute as a potential first crack. Such a move could signal that big tech has overbuilt its data center capacity, undercutting the industry's assumption that demand will absorb every GPU.

title: AI post-training startup Bespoke Labs raises $40M in funding
blurb: Bespoke Labs raised $40M to streamline the post-training phase of AI development, with a $31.75M Series A led by Wing VC and an earlier $8.25M round backed by Google DeepMind chief scientist Jeff Dean. Its platform generates reinforcement learning environments and sandboxes them for training, using tools like the open-source GEPA project to automate prompt optimization.

title: SPEARBench: A Benchmark for Naturalness Evaluation in Streaming Speech-to-Speech Language Models
blurb: SPEARBench is a benchmark that evaluates whether streaming speech-to-speech language models behave naturally in conversation, measuring latency, turn-taking, prosody, dialect consistency, and interpersonal stance from question-answer interactions. Built on the Seamless Interaction corpus with human answers as a reference, it finds that current models hit high signal quality and low ASR error while still diverging from human behavior in latency, overlap, dialect preservation, and emotional adaptation.

title: Using Process Mining to Generate AI Agents from Software Engineering Process Records
blurb: This paper proposes a method that combines object-centric, imperative, and declarative process mining over event logs extracted from software repositories to discover project-specific AI agent roles and generate matching agent specifications. The approach grounds roles in a predefined SE role vocabulary and repository behavior, and was tested on an open-source project through functional tests and an exploratory user study measuring alignment with human expectations.

title: Multi-Large Language Model Orchestrated Severity Assessment of Clinical Records (MOSAIC)
blurb: MOSAIC is a two-phase agentic LLM framework for scoring disease severity from structured EHR data, tested on type 2 diabetes across a synthetic cohort of nearly 5,000 patients. Its locally deployable open-weight pipeline matched a proprietary one (weighted kappa 0.773), separated all-cause mortality across severity tiers, and diverged from a frozen rubric, indicating the agents reason beyond fixed rules.

title: Sources: Beijing recently held meetings with Alibaba, ByteDance, Z.ai, and others to discuss restricting overseas access to advanced open and closed AI models (Fanny Potkin/Reuters)
blurb: Beijing recently met with Alibaba, ByteDance, Z.ai, and other domestic labs to discuss restricting overseas access to their advanced open and closed AI models. The move would reverse the export strategy that made Chinese open-weight models the default global alternative to US frontier labs, from DeepSeek to Qwen. For founders who built products on cheap, permissively licensed Chinese models, the supply you treated as a commodity is now a geopolitical lever, and access could tighten with little warning.

title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
blurb: Forterra says more than 100 of its self-driving ATVs have been fighting in Ukraine for the past nine months, in what it calls the largest combat deployment of autonomous ground vehicles by any U.S. defense tech company. Aerial drones created no-go zones where any movement can draw fire from above, so Ukrainian strategists turned to ground autonomy to haul supplies and evacuate wounded without exposing soldiers. Forterra's gas-powered Lancers carry 750kg, three times what Ukraine's battery-powered UGVs manage, and the fix that made them work in the field was adding a Starlink antenna.

title: Klarna seeks U.S. bank charter in latest push beyond buy now, pay later
blurb: Klarna applied for a U.S. bank charter, seeking an FDIC-backed subsidiary in Utah as it pushes beyond buy now, pay later toward being a full consumer bank. Owning a charter lets fintechs fund loans with their own deposits instead of costlier wholesale financing, offer checking and credit cards directly, and stop leaning on partner banks like WebBank. Klarna joins Mercury and a wave of fintech and crypto firms filing for charters, a sign that the old partner-bank playbook is now seen as a ceiling rather than a shortcut.

title: Sources: DeepSeek is in the early stages of developing its own AI chip designed for inference, in a bid to reduce its reliance on Nvidia and Huawei hardware (Reuters)
blurb: DeepSeek is developing its own inference chip to cut its dependence on Nvidia and Huawei silicon, according to Reuters sources. The effort is early-stage and inference-only, which is the easier design target and the one that matters most for a company serving models at scale rather than training new ones. It signals that China's frontier labs increasingly see custom silicon, not just model weights, as the part of the stack they need to control as US export limits keep tightening.

title: Where Robinhood's 7% yield comes from
blurb: Robinhood launched Earn, a 7% APY yield product built on a Morpho vault that lends overcollateralized to institutions, available to all 27 million users in the core app and insured by Lloyd's of London. Roughly half the yield is marketing spend today, but as USDG float grows on the treasuries it buys, that subsidy should convert into native yield the stablecoin funds itself. Combine that with stock tokens users can borrow against, and Robinhood starts to look like a decentralized prime broker, with more control over its stablecoin than Coinbase has over USDC.

title: North American Startup Funding Shattered Records In First Half Of 2026, Driven By AI
blurb: North American startups pulled in $392 billion in the first half of 2026, an all-time record built almost entirely on AI megarounds. Anthropic alone raised $65 billion at a $965 billion valuation, accounting for roughly half of Q2's $137.2 billion total. Deal count stayed well below recent highs, so this is capital concentration, not a broad funding recovery. The exit window opened too, with SpaceX's IPO and its record-setting acquisition of Cursor. If you're raising outside the AI frontier, the headline numbers describe a market you aren't in.

title: GLM 5.2 and the coming AI margin collapse
blurb: Frontier labs earn roughly 90% gross margins on inference because training is a one-time cost while inference is where the money is made. GLM 5.2 from Z.ai is the first open-weights model the author finds hard to distinguish from Opus or GPT-5.5, which means that fat inference margin now has a near-free substitute anyone can self-host. If open weights keep closing the quality gap, the amortization model that makes frontier AI profitable starts to break, and the coming margin collapse lands on the labs charging premium API rates.

title: TeraWulf signs 20-year data center lease with Anthropic expected to generate $19 billion in revenue
blurb: Bitcoin miner TeraWulf signed a 20-year lease with Anthropic expected to generate about $19 billion, and its stock jumped 17% on the day. The deal covers a purpose-built AI campus in Hawesville, Kentucky, delivering 401 MW of IT load by early 2028, and it pushed WULF up over 117% year-to-date. Nearly every bitcoin miner is now bolting on AI infrastructure to trade volatile block rewards for predictable long-term revenue, and the frontier labs are locking up compute capacity years ahead of when they actually need it.

title: OpenRouter: Chinese AI models have drawn 30%+ of token use by US companies each week since February 8, peaking at 46%, up from 11% over the previous 12 months (Kai Nicol-Schwarz/CNBC)
blurb: US companies now route more than 30% of their weekly AI token usage to Chinese models, a share that has held since February 8 and peaked at 46%, up from just 11% over the prior 12 months. The pull is cost and openness, as open-weight models from Chinese labs close the quality gap while undercutting frontier pricing. If you sell an AI product, your customers are already comfortable running someone else's cheaper model, and that comfort sets the ceiling on what you can charge for the frontier.

title: ChatGPT commands 92% of AI referral traffic. Here’s what 6.77 million sessions reveal.
blurb: ChatGPT now commands 92.4% of trackable LLM referral traffic across 6.77 million sessions, up from roughly 84% six months ago, while Perplexity, Gemini, and Copilot collapsed toward the leader. That concentration makes "AI visibility" a misleading target. If you aren't prioritizing ChatGPT specifically, you are optimizing for an abstraction. The other lesson is volatility: a single model tweak cut ChatGPT referrals in half almost overnight last November before traffic rebounded to new highs, so treat AI referral traffic as a channel one vendor can swing at will, not a stable acquisition source.

title: China's Alibaba bans Anthropic AI for employees after 'distillation attack' accusation
blurb: Alibaba will bar employees from Anthropic's Claude Code starting July 10, flagging it as high-risk software and ordering staff onto its own Qoder assistant. The ban answers Anthropic's June letter to the U.S. Senate accusing Alibaba of "the largest known distillation attack" to date, and follows reports of hidden Claude code that detects China-based users. For founders, this is the AI supply chain splitting along geopolitical lines, with Anthropic now closing the third-country loopholes that let firms like Ant and ByteDance reach Claude through Singapore entities and expensed personal subscriptions.

title: TeraWulf Signs $19 Billion Lease With Anthropic for AI-Infrastructure Campus
blurb: TeraWulf signed a $19B lease with Anthropic to build an AI-infrastructure campus in Kentucky, one of the largest compute commitments yet from a frontier lab.
