blurb: A walkthrough of AWS Lambda durable functions against Step Functions and MWAA, using one workflow implemented three ways plus five scenarios to build a decision framework. Durable functions put orchestration in your application code via a checkpoint-and-replay SDK, running up to a year and debuggable with local stack traces, while Step Functions keeps state machines outside your code and MWAA handles DAG-based data pipelines. The practical split: application-level orchestration, cross-service orchestration, and data-pipeline orchestration respectively.

title: OpenAI can generate ChatGPT Ads for you
blurb: OpenAI rolled out a ChatGPT Ads feature that generates ad variations from an advertiser's website and campaign settings, then lets the marketer review, edit, and activate before delivery. The move lowers the barrier to launching on ChatGPT's ad platform and points to OpenAI monetizing inventory faster, though marketers still own the brand-safety and ROI review on anything the model drafts.

title: Phishing poses as big-brand job interview to steal Google accounts
blurb: A phishing campaign is impersonating more than 30 major brands, including Adobe, Netflix, Coca-Cola, and OpenAI, in fake marketing-role job interviews that steal Google account credentials from marketing professionals. The operation abuses legitimate platforms like PeopleForce and Salesforce Marketing Cloud through nested redirects, and has run for at least five months using real recruiters' names and photos to build trust.

title: Self-Promotional Content Works—Until It Backfires (AI SEO Experiment)
blurb: Ahrefs ran a four-month experiment publishing 34 self-promotional pages across 5 domains and analyzing 9,886 answers from ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, and Copilot to test whether "best [category]" listicles actually get a brand recommended. The finding echoes Lily Ray's earlier work: getting cited as a source and getting recommended are different outcomes, and AI assistants frequently pull a listicle in behind the scenes without crediting it or endorsing its top pick. Track both metrics separately, since a page can be found and used without ever surfacing your brand as the answer.

title: ChatGPT commands 92% of AI referral traffic. Here’s what 6.77 million sessions reveal.
blurb: New data from Previsible's analysis of 6.77 million LLM-driven sessions shows ChatGPT now drives 92.4% of trackable AI referral traffic, as monthly LLM sessions grew 9.9x to 644,478 in May 2026. The study also documents severe volatility, including a single month where a model change cut ChatGPT referrals in half, so teams chasing AI visibility should prioritize ChatGPT and plan for traffic that a vendor tweak can halve overnight.

title: Reddit’s AI is hunting brands that dress marketing slop up as honest opinions
blurb: Reddit rolled out upgraded LLM-based detection to catch brands seeding fake posts and comments through generative engine optimization, the practice of planting content so ChatGPT and Gemini surface it as organic recommendations. The systems now block 23M spam views daily and revoke nearly 2M inauthentic votes per day, cutting user exposure to spam roughly 20% between January and March 2026.

title: How To Make AEO and GEO Profitable
blurb: AI-referred visitors convert at 8.3 times the rate of traditional traffic and generate 7 times more revenue per visitor, but most teams optimize AEO and GEO for mentions and citations rather than the conversions those mentions should drive. Neil Patel's analysis of 100-plus campaigns points to four failure modes, chief among them measuring AI visibility like keyword rankings and running it in a silo. The fix is retrieval-ready content, authority signals, multi-channel distribution, and conversion systems built for low-click environments, sequenced over 90 days.

title: X wants to keep your video edits in-house, and it’s starting with captions and custom backgrounds
blurb: X released a redesigned iOS Video Editor with built-in multilingual captions and a green-screen background swap, features creators previously needed third-party apps to access. The move cuts friction that pushed video off-platform and follows X's Live Studio launch and million-dollar creator payout, all aimed at keeping content creation inside the app and boosting native video volume.

title: A Google Ads targeting tactic that cut invalid clicks by 50%
blurb: An agency ran 540 Google-defined audiences set to "Targeting" across a client's Search campaigns and cut invalid clicks by 50%, restoring profitable conversion rates. The client, a book-editing service, was seeing 60-80% invalid-click rates and bot-like GA4 sessions even after Google claimed it had filtered the fraudulent traffic. Advertisers are projected to lose $172B a year to ad fraud by 2028, and the case study argues audience targeting narrows delivery enough to starve competitor and bot clicks that Google's own filters miss.

title: Why Meta AI could become search’s sleeping giant
blurb: Meta AI reached one billion monthly active users by May 2025 and sits inside Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp, apps that logged 3.56 billion daily active people last quarter. That distribution lets Meta intercept the first search at the point of discovery, letting users ask about a product they just saw on Instagram without ever opening Google.

title: Paid media is becoming an SEO investment in AI search
blurb: Paid media is now a direct input to how AI systems rank and recommend brands, because RAG engines like Perplexity and ChatGPT pull from the sources marketers can fund. G2 reviews, YouTube sponsorships, and native UGC feed the semantic consensus LLMs use to judge sentiment and use cases, making a $250 G2 review incentive the modern equivalent of buying a high-DA backlink. The takeaway for growth teams is to treat spend on reviews, creator partnerships, and third-party mentions as SEO infrastructure, and to track where the brand already surfaces in AI answers versus competitors.

title: X Launches Live Studio, Backs Livestreaming Push With $1M Creator Fund
blurb: X launched Live Studio, a livestreaming command center inside Creator Studio that lets creators schedule streams, gate access to verified accounts or subscribers, and track concurrent viewers, viewer geography, and devices in real time. The platform is backing the push with $1M in creator payouts for the coming cycle, though livestreaming stays locked behind X Premium and payout structure remains undisclosed.

title: X rolls out updated video editor, including green screen recording
blurb: X updated its native video editor with green screen recording and multi-language captioning, part of a broader set of features aimed at keeping video creation inside the app. The additions push X closer to TikTok and Instagram Reels as a self-contained production tool, reducing marketers' reliance on external editing software before posting.

title: ‘Tough pill to swallow': LadBible boss on the traffic hit from Meta's feed shake-up
blurb: Meta's shift to promote creator content over publisher links triggered a 41% drop in LBG Media's indirect revenue, and the LadBible owner's shares fell as much as 40% in a day after a second profit warning in two months. Publishers from LBG to the owners of Marie Claire and the Daily Mail now face rebuilding business models that leaned on Facebook and Instagram referral traffic.

title: The New Shape of Search: How Conversational AI Recomposes Information Seeking
blurb: A cross-surface panel study linking real assistant conversations to the same users' searches finds that conversational AI recomposes search rather than replacing it, with search still woven through roughly three-quarters of within-episode transitions and users returning to the search box over the assistant 70/30 after reading a page. Brief asks terminate in the assistant about 72% of the time while longer, more articulated queries scaffold into multi-step journeys, and explicit verification follows only about 1% of episodes.

title: WordPress Drops to 41.5% Market Share, but Three Datasets Tell Different Stories About What's Going On
blurb: WordPress now powers 41.5% of the web, down from a 43.6% peak in early 2025, with more share lost in the first half of 2026 than in all of the prior year. Coverage has pinned the slide on the WP Engine v. Automattic fight, but W3Techs data shows WordPress actually peaked after that conflict began and didn't start declining until Q2 2025.

title: How to measure prompt-level visibility in AI search
blurb: AI search has no fixed rankings, so measuring brand visibility means tracking probabilistic inclusion across conversations rather than a "position 1." The recommended approach: build a prompt library of 200 to 500 queries mapped to buying-journey intent instead of a keyword list, then analyze prompt clusters rather than single questions, since "best CRM software" and "CRM for manufacturers with field sales teams" surface different recommendations. The metric that matters shifts from "do we rank?" to "how often are we included across the prompts that drive purchases?

title: YouTube Partners With Mark Rober’s Crunch Labs to Launch Free Science Curriculum
blurb: YouTube is partnering with Mark Rober's Crunch Labs and the National Science Teaching Association to launch Class CrunchLabs, a free, standards-aligned science curriculum for grades 3 through 8 rolling out for back-to-school 2026 with 1,000-plus videos in 34 languages. The move deepens YouTube's push to own academic content and gives brands a creator-led template for scaled, credentialed education distribution rather than one-off sponsorships.

title: 6 SEO priorities to rethink for AI search
blurb: AI search rewards different SEO work than traditional rankings, shifting emphasis toward brand-entity consistency across Wikipedia, LinkedIn, and Crunchbase, deep topical content clusters, and tighter PR-SEO collaboration on earned media. The author argues thin content footprints and keyword-by-keyword planning are far more exposed now, so teams should audit for topic-coverage gaps and build author entities that earn faster AI citations.

title: Identifying Deceptive Patterns Across Three Age Groups: A Heuristic-Based Cognitive Walkthrough Study of Mobile Apps
blurb: This paper runs a heuristic-based cognitive walkthrough of 30 mobile apps across 6 categories to map how deceptive patterns are tailored to teens, adults, and older adults. It found that 93% of apps use nagging, entertainment apps carry the most deceptive patterns, and entertainment apps aimed at older adults lean on sneaking patterns more than those for younger users. The authors argue for age-specific design guidelines, a useful prompt for PMs auditing their own consent flows and upsell nudges for who they quietly disadvantage.

title: Rating the Pitch, Not the Product: User Evaluations of LLMs Reflect Expectations More Than Performance
blurb: A controlled study of 162 users found that showing a landing page that oversold or undersold an LLM's capability shifted how favorably people rated it, even though the model was identical and task performance did not change. Impression changes after use were predicted by whether the model met expectations and how confident users felt, not by the quality of the work produced. For PMs, the takeaway is that preference data and public leaderboards measure expectation management as much as model quality, so onboarding framing and perceived confidence are levers on satisfaction scores independent of actual output.

title: X wants to keep your video edits in-house, and it’s starting with captions and custom backgrounds
blurb: X released a redesigned Video Editor and Recorder for iOS that adds in-app multilingual captions and a green-screen background swap, features that previously required third-party editing apps. The move follows X's Live Studio launch and million-dollar creator payout, part of a broader push to keep creators inside the app and reduce the friction that keeps casual users from posting.

title: The New Shape of Search: How Conversational AI Recomposes Information Seeking
blurb: This paper links real conversations with major AI assistants to the same users' searches, then reconstructs the full seeking episode rather than a single query. It finds conversational AI adds a layer instead of replacing search: most episodes end in place, but roughly a third scaffold into longer multi-step journeys, and shape tracks opening-ask length more than task type. Search still runs through three-quarters of within-episode transitions, and explicit verification follows only about 1% of episodes.

title: Vitalik Buterin proposes ‘Extremely Lean’ Ethereum, shrinking the chain to near-zero state with ZK proofs
blurb: Vitalik Buterin proposed an "Extremely Lean" redesign of Ethereum's Beacon Chain that shifts state management to validators and replaces per-epoch balance updates with a single daily ZK-STARK proof, shrinking onchain validator state to roughly 6 bytes. Buterin says the two-step plan could scale Ethereum to millions of validators and would take around four years to fully implement.

title: Vitalik Buterin Outlines 'Lean Ethereum' Roadmap, a Three-to-Four-Year Protocol Overhaul
blurb: Vitalik Buterin published takeaways on "Lean Ethereum," a three-to-four-year roadmap he calls Ethereum's third major iteration after the Merge, in which nearly every core protocol component gets replaced. Key changes include swapping transaction re-execution for recursive STARK verification, moving to quantum-safe cryptography, decoupling data availability from finality for one- or two-round finality, and adding multidimensional gas.

title: Coinbase secures UK investment services license to add derivatives and equities trading
blurb: Coinbase received authorization from the UK's Financial Conksial Conduct Authority to offer investment services, letting institutional traders access crypto, equity, and commodity perpetual futures while retail users trade equities on the platform for the first time. The license sits alongside Coinbase's existing UK e-money license and crypto registration, marking its largest local product expansion ahead of a full UK crypto regime slated for October 2027.

title: Prompt Injection Attacks Trick AI Agents Into Making Crypto Payments
blurb: Zscaler identified two crypto-focused prompt injection campaigns that manipulate AI agents into sending payments to attacker-controlled wallets, one hiding payment instructions in fake Python-package API docs via SEO poisoning and another typosquatting DeFi tracker DeBank. In the firm's tests, 4 of 26 evaluated LLMs, including Llama 3.3 70B and Gemini 2.5 Pro, executed the fraudulent transfers when running as autonomous browsing agents.

title: Robinhood accelerates global expansion with chain mainnet, stock tokens, agentic trading, and DeFi products
blurb: Robinhood launched the public mainnet of Robinhood Chain, an Arbitrum-built L2, alongside tokenized stocks now tradable 24/7 in more than 120 countries and usable as DeFi collateral. Day-one partners include Uniswap, which is deploying a dedicated AMM for public liquidity, plus Alchemy, BitGo, and Chainlink integrations, positioning Robinhood to route its retail brokerage users directly onto an onchain trading venue.

title: Klarna seeks U.S. bank charter in latest push beyond buy now, pay later
blurb: Klarna applied to U.S. federal and state regulators to establish Klarna Bank USA, an FDIC-backed subsidiary chartered in Utah and led by former Milestone Bank CEO Gary Harding. Owning a charter would let the Swedish fintech fund loans with customer deposits instead of wholesale financing and cut reliance on partners like WebBank, joining Mercury and a wave of fintech and crypto firms seeking bank charters.

title: Coinbase secures UK authorization to offer traditional investments alongside crypto
blurb: Coinbase secured UK regulatory approval to offer equities and derivatives alongside crypto, letting institutional traders access crypto, equity, and commodity perpetual futures while UK retail customers trade stocks on the platform for the first time. The license, which sits alongside Coinbase's existing e-money and cryptoasset registrations, advances its "Everything Exchange" strategy and provides a regulated path to expand before the UK's full crypto framework takes effect in October 2027.

title: Bitcoin miner TeraWulf soars on a $19 billion AI data-center lease with Anthropic
blurb: TeraWulf, a bitcoin miner turned AI landlord, signed a 20-year lease with Anthropic to host about 401 megawatts of AI data-center capacity at its Kentucky campus starting in 2027, generating roughly $19B in contracted revenue and sending shares up as much as 19% before closing near 4%. The deal exceeds TeraWulf's ~$12B market value and adds to a broader shift of miners trading volatile block rewards for steadier AI hosting income.

title: JPMorgan's JLTXX Tokenized Money Market Fund AUM Grows 250% in a Month on Ethereum
blurb: JPMorgan's JLTXX tokenized money market fund grew its onchain AUM roughly 250% in a month, reaching $695 million in TVL seven weeks after launching on Ethereum with $200 million. The growth reflects demand for GENIUS Act-compliant reserve assets, with JLTXX now backing USDG's reserves alongside BlackRock's BUIDL and Superstate's STBXX as banks compete to supply yield-bearing stablecoin collateral.

title: BonkDAO loses $20 million following ‘malicious governance proposal’ attack
blurb: BonkDAO lost $20 million after an attacker pushed through a malicious governance proposal, draining funds that on-chain trackers followed to several centralized exchanges. Upbit, the South Korea-based exchange, suspended BONK deposits and withdrawals in response, while the incident renews scrutiny of governance mechanisms that let a single passed proposal move treasury assets.

title: CertiK Says H1 2026 Web3 Losses Topped $1.31B, Up 28% Excluding Bybit Baseline
blurb: CertiK's Hack3D H1 2026 report counted more than $1.31B in Web3 losses across 344 incidents, and while that headline total is down 46.8% year-over-year, the drop is an artifact of last year's $1.45B Bybit hack. Stripping out that outlier, losses ran roughly 28% higher than H1 2025, with wallet compromise the costliest vector at over $444M across 33 incidents.

title: dYdX Rebrands to Arcus, Partners With Robinhood for Tokenized Stocks
blurb: dYdX rebranded to Arcus and launched a joint trading product with Robinhood, offering 24/7 access to 95 tokenized stocks and 35 RWA perps at 0% fees via RFQ execution. The move distributes the protocol to Robinhood's 25M users after dYdX's v4 chain ceded perp market share to faster rivals like Hyperliquid, betting that speed and UX matter more than maximal decentralization.

title: German Savings and Co-op Banks Launch Native Crypto Trading
blurb: Germany's Sparkassen and Volksbanken, which together serve tens of millions of retail customers, are rolling out native crypto trading inside their existing banking apps. The integration lets Germans buy and sell crypto without routing through third-party exchanges, removing a key onboarding step and giving regulated incumbents direct exposure to retail crypto flows.

title: Circle’s USDC is leaving Tether behind in the stablecoin volume race, new data from Visa shows
blurb: Circle's USDC accounted for roughly 70% of adjusted stablecoin transaction volume in the first half of 2026, widening its lead over Tether's USDT at about 25%, according to Visa's onchain dashboard. Adjusted volume hit a record $1.79T in June, up 63% from May and 125% year-over-year, as banks including Standard Chartered and BNY built payment and settlement services on USDC rather than their own rails.

title: Post-Quantum Migration and Abandoned Coins
blurb: Coinbase's Quantum Advisory Council published a framework for migrating Bitcoin and other chains to post-quantum cryptography, warning that current ECDSA signatures become vulnerable once quantum computers reach sufficient scale. The proposal flags abandoned coins with exposed public keys, including early Satoshi-era wallets, as unmigratable and proposes freezing or sunsetting them before a quantum-capable attacker can drain the funds.

title: BitMine Adds $73 Million in ETH, Pushing Holdings to 4.8% of Supply
blurb: BitMine Immersion Technologies bought 42,197 ETH worth roughly $73 million over the past week, lifting its total to 5,742,237 ETH, or about 4.8% of Ethereum's circulating supply. The purchase keeps the Tom Lee-chaired treasury company, now the largest corporate ETH holder, on pace toward its stated goal of controlling 5% of total supply against $11.1 billion in combined holdings.

title: Ripple Secures Full MiCA License, Completing EU Compliance
blurb: Ripple received full Crypto Asset Service Provider authorization from Luxembourg's CSSF, completing its MiCA requirements and clearing it to offer regulated crypto payments, custody, and related services across all 30 EEA countries. The approval lands after MiCA's transitional grandfathering period ended July 1, placing Ripple among a small group of firms with full authorization and pushing its global license count past 75.

title: Galaxy delivers 133 MW of critical IT load to CoreWeave as Helios bitcoin mine turns AI hub
blurb: Galaxy Digital completed Phase I of its Helios campus in West Texas, delivering 133 MW of critical IT load to CoreWeave under a 15-year lease that marks the former bitcoin mine's conversion to AI infrastructure. CoreWeave has committed to 526 MW across three phases, the site's full approved capacity, expected to generate more than $1B in average annual revenue.

title: The coin nobody owns
blurb: OpenUSD is a dollar-backed stablecoin operated by an independent company, OpenStandard, backed by Visa, Mastercard, Stripe, Coinbase, BlackRock, ICE, and roughly a hundred banks and processors. Unlike Circle and Tether, it shares reserve yield with distributors, runs neutral governance under a separate board, and charges zero mint-and-burn fees. This piece argues it isn't fighting the incumbents for existing stablecoin volume, but targeting bank and capital-markets flows that have stayed off-chain entirely.

title: Two traders sue Polymarket over disputed resolution of Strategy bitcoin sale market
blurb: Two Polymarket traders sued the prediction market platform over its resolution of a market on whether Strategy sold bitcoin, which resolved "No" despite the company's SEC filing disclosing a sale of 32 BTC between May 26 and May 31. The plaintiffs allege the outcome was wrongly settled, raising questions about how Polymarket's UMA-based oracle handles disputes when on-chain resolution conflicts with public filings.

title: Paradigm leads $5.5 million seed round in M1X Global to expand tokenized sovereign debt platform
blurb: Paradigm led a $5.5 million seed round in M1X Global, the firm that helped the Republic of the Marshall Islands issue its onchain sovereign bond USDM1. The funding backs M1X's push to expand its tokenized sovereign debt platform, extending crypto's real-world-asset trade into government issuance rather than just Treasuries and private credit.

title: Strategy sells 3,588 BTC for $216 million, with total bitcoin holdings still underwater
blurb: Strategy sold 3,588 BTC for $216 million, its first bitcoin sale after years of accumulation, while its total holdings of more than 4% of the 21 million supply cap remain underwater at a cost basis above current prices. The position is now worth around $52.3 billion, leaving the company's aggregate bitcoin bet at an unrealized loss despite the divestment.

title: Russia's largest bank plans crypto wallet launch as Moscow clears market path
blurb: Sberbank, Russia's largest bank, plans to launch a crypto wallet and digital depository inside its Sberbank Online and SberInvestments apps by December, pending the "On Digital Currency and Digital Rights" bill taking effect September 1. The move follows years of Bank of Russia opposition and creates licensed rails for trading, custody, and cross-border settlements, with non-qualified investors capped at roughly 300,000 rubles ($3,800) per year.

title: Crypto Card Spending Surpasses $800M Monthly
blurb: Crypto-linked debit and credit card spending crossed $800M in monthly volume, as networks like Visa and Mastercard route more stablecoin and token payments through consumer-facing card programs. The figure signals that on-chain balances are increasingly being spent at point of sale rather than held, giving issuers and payment processors a measurable stream of real-world crypto transaction fees.

title: President Trump’s strategic bitcoin reserve hits a legal and jurisdictional snag: Bloomberg
blurb: President Trump's planned strategic bitcoin reserve is running into legal and jurisdictional obstacles, according to Bloomberg reporting, complicating the mechanism for acquiring and custodying the government's holdings. The snag raises questions about which agency has authority over the reserve and whether new statutory backing is needed, slowing a policy that markets had treated as a structural tailwind for bitcoin demand.

title: Robinhood launches blockchain designed for real-world assets
blurb: Robinhood launched Robinhood Chain, an Arbitrum-based L2 built for tokenized real-world assets, alongside Stock Tokens for 24/7 trading in 120 countries, USDG lending via Robinhood Earn, and perpetual futures access through Lighter. The chain integrates Alchemy, BitGo, and Chainlink, extending Robinhood's pivot from a retail trading app toward an onchain financial platform spanning brokerage, banking, and DeFi.

title: Judge revives fraud claim against Barry Silbert, DCG in Genesis Yield lawsuit
blurb: A federal judge revived a fraud claim against Digital Currency Group and CEO Barry Silbert in the class action over Genesis Yield, the lending program that paid customers interest on deposited crypto. The ruling lets plaintiffs proceed on allegations that DCG misrepresented Genesis's financial health before the lender collapsed and froze withdrawals in late 2022.

title: Russia’s largest bank Sber targets early December crypto wallet launch: report
blurb: Sberbank, Russia's largest bank, plans to launch a crypto wallet and digital depository inside its Sber apps by early December, pending the country's new digital asset law, according to a report. The move would put custody and secondary-market access for digital assets directly into a state-controlled banking platform serving over 100 million Russian customers.

title: BonkDAO Treasury Drained of $20M via Malicious Proposal
blurb: BonkDAO, the DAO tied to Solana memecoin BONK, said an estimated $20M in BONK was drained from its treasury through a malicious governance proposal rather than a smart-contract bug. The DAO has identified the exchange wallets used to buy BONK ahead of the vote, notified law enforcement, and is working with exchanges, bridges, and the Solana Foundation to recover funds.

title: Summer Finance Drained of $6M in Flash Loan Exploit
blurb: Summer Finance, the DeFi yield aggregator known as SummerFi, was drained of roughly $6 million in DAI on Monday after an attacker used a $65.4 million flash loan to inflate the reported assets of its Lazy Summer vaults and redeem more than they deposited. Researchers traced the root cause to an accounting flaw in how the Fleet Commander architecture priced Ark strategy holdings, exploitable within a single transaction using legitimate protocol functions.

title: Strategy Sells 3,588 Bitcoin for $216M to Fund Dividend Payments
blurb: Strategy sold 3,588 BTC for roughly $216M between June 29 and July 5, using the proceeds to cover second-quarter dividends on its STRF, STRE, STRK and STRD preferred securities and to replenish its USD reserve. The sale left the $1.25B Bitcoin Monetization Program authorization untouched, and Strategy still holds 843,775 BTC at a $63.69B cost basis; MSTR fell about 2% pre-market.

title: Bitcoin's U.S. reserve still a work-in-progress as federal agencies hash it out
blurb: The White House said it is still evaluating the "best structure" for a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, with Treasury and Commerce both vying to house the fund and Congress yet to advance the legislation advisers say is required. Trump's March 2025 executive order launched the effort, but presidential orders lack the force of law, and midterm losses could stall any formalizing bill indefinitely.

title: Securitize eyes acquisitions with $400 million war chest after going public, CEO says
blurb: Securitize began trading on the NYSE Thursday after completing its SPAC merger with Cantor Equity Partners II, and CEO Carlos Domingo said the firm will spend part of its $400 million balance sheet on acquisitions. Rather than buying rivals, Securitize plans to acquire complementary businesses to build a one-stop shop for institutional tokenization, having already issued roughly $4.4 billion in tokenized assets for clients including BlackRock and Apollo.

title: President Trump says he’s a ‘big crypto guy,’ while distancing himself from family’s crypto ventures
blurb: President Trump called cryptocurrency "powerful" at the launch of Trump investment accounts for children, while publicly distancing himself from his family's crypto ventures. The comments continue his pro-crypto positioning even as his family's World Liberty Financial and other token projects draw conflict-of-interest scrutiny.

title: Kraken platform Payward acquires payments firm Reap
blurb: Payward, the parent company of Kraken, acquired stablecoin payments firm Reap to expand its Payward Services B2B infrastructure platform, with the deal announced in May and completed July 1. The acquisition adds embedded card issuance, cross-border payments, and stablecoin-based treasury management to Payward's stack, letting partners settle against on-chain liquidity through a single API instead of correspondent banks.

title: DeFi protocol Summer.fi halts Lazy Summer vaults after $6 million exploit
blurb: Summer.fi paused its Ethereum-based Lazy Summer vaults after an attacker drained roughly $6 million, using a Morpho-sourced flash loan to manipulate the automated USDC vaults' accounting logic and redeem artificially inflated assets for profit. The protocol held $22 million in TVL before the exploit, per DeFiLlama, and its SUMR token fell more than 18% after the incident surfaced.

title: Pay for APIs with USDC via x402 and Apify
blurb: Apify integrated x402, the HTTP 402-based payment protocol, letting developers pay for API and scraping calls with USDC on a per-request basis instead of prepaid subscriptions. The setup routes stablecoin micropayments through the standard 402 "Payment Required" response, giving AI agents a native way to settle for compute and data access without accounts or API keys.

title: Ripple's preliminary crypto asset provider license in Luxembourg upgraded to fully compliant
blurb: Ripple received a full MiCA Crypto-Asset Service Provider license from Luxembourg's CSSF, clearing it to passport regulated cryptoasset services across all 30 European Economic Area countries. The approval puts Ripple among a small group of firms fully authorized before MiCA's July 1 deadline, while unlicensed players like Binance must now halt EEA operations.

title: Circle CEO on Why OpenUSD Won't Unseat USDC
blurb: Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire argued that OpenUSD won't displace USDC because stablecoin networks are winner-take-most businesses whose moats compound through developer integrations and liquidity network effects built over nearly a decade. He pointed to USDC's thousands of integrated services and interoperability protocols like CCTP and Gateway as the accumulated advantage a new entrant can't quickly replicate.

title: ENS Co-Founder Proposes Delegating 5M ENS Tokens to Reform DAO Governance
blurb: ENS co-founder Alex Van de Sande proposed delegating 5M ENS tokens from the DAO's dormant community treasury to individual participants, targeting fellow co-founder Nick Johnson's roughly 3.26M delegated tokens that give him quorum to pass or block any proposal. The move follows weeks of governance conflict, including Johnson using that voting power to halt a Security Council renewal vote.

title: Orcaella: Hybrid Fault Tolerance with Client-Selectable Finality Latency
blurb: This paper introduces Orcaella, a Byzantine fault tolerance protocol that lets clients trade finality latency for resilience within a mixed Byzantine-and-crash fault model. The authors prove a tight bound of n ≥ 5f+3c+1 for a 2-message-delay commit and add a slower 4-message-delay path that absorbs extra alive-but-corrupt faults. For n=99, one configuration tolerates 22% of replicas failing for liveness, 16% equivocating for 1-RTT safety, and 54% for 2-RTT safety.

title: Strategy selling hundreds of millions worth of bitcoin raises question about its capital-allocation playbook
blurb: Strategy sold 3,588 BTC for roughly $216 million just days after buying 3,657 BTC at higher prices, netting an increase of only 69 coins despite deploying about $20 million in fresh capital. The company also booked an $8.31 billion unrealized loss on its 843,775 BTC holdings for Q2, pressuring the capital-allocation model that funds dividends on its high-yield Stretch preferred stock.

title: Bitmine added another $74 million in ether as Tom Lee bets on Clarity Act boost
blurb: Bitmine Immersion bought 42,197 ether worth roughly $74 million last week, lifting its holdings to 5.74 million ETH, or 4.8% of circulating supply, as chairman Tom Lee tied ETH's outperformance to expectations that the Clarity Act will pass. The accumulation contrasts with Strategy, which sold about $216 million in bitcoin to raise cash amid dividend obligations and the market downturn.

title: Michael Saylor's Strategy dramatically ups pace of bitcoin sales, raising $216 million
blurb: Strategy sold 3,588 bitcoin for roughly $216 million last week, cutting its holdings to 843,775 BTC and refilling the dollar reserve it uses to pay preferred-stock dividends. The pace marks a sharp shift from the 32 BTC it sold a month earlier, and MSTR shares fell 2% pre-market as bitcoin slid back to $61,900.

title: Upbit, Naver stock-swap deal faces second delay amid proposed crypto law debate
blurb: Dunamu, the parent company of South Korean exchange Upbit, and Naver Financial pushed their $9.9B stock-swap merger back to Dec. 31, 2026, the deal's second delay after an earlier move from June to September. The companies cited South Korea's pending Digital Asset Basic Act, which may include a clause capping any single shareholder's stake in a domestic exchange at 20%.

title: Binance taps into Bitcoin holders’ hunger for yield with new covered call yield play
blurb: Binance launched BTC Yield, a Bitcoin-denominated product inside Binance Earn that runs a systematic covered-call strategy, collecting option premiums and sharing most with depositors via weekly BTC payouts and a compounding BTCY unit. The product accepts only BTC as collateral and returns are not guaranteed, since gains cap out when bitcoin rallies and the written call options are exercised.

title: World Cup sends prediction market volumes soaring to record highs
blurb: Kalshi processed more than $31B in notional volume during June, a 70% jump from May, while Polymarket's international exchange set a monthly record above $10.8B as the FIFA World Cup drove prediction market activity to all-time highs. Robinhood and Susquehanna's new venture Rothera cleared $2B in its debut month, already capturing 7% of US prediction market volume.

title: AVAX One CEO Jolie Kahn Resigns Days After Stock Crash Warning
blurb: AVAX One Technology CEO Jolie Kahn resigned effective July 3 by mutual agreement, with COO Pete Wylie taking over as interim while ZRG Partners runs a search for a permanent successor. The Nasdaq-listed bitcoin miner and Avalanche treasury holder disclosed the exit the same day as its July 6 deadline to reclaim compliance with the exchange's $1.00 minimum bid price rule.

title: Ethereum developers embrace Vitalik Buterin's long-term vision but urge quicker execution
blurb: Ethereum's leading researchers broadly endorsed Vitalik Buterin's updated "Lean Ethereum" strawmap, which centers recursive STARKs, privacy, and quantum-resistant cryptography as the network's multi-year direction. Several prominent figures, including StarkWare's Eli Ben-Sasson, called the proposed three-to-four-year timeline too slow and urged faster execution, particularly on quantum readiness.

title: Vitalik Buterin proposes ‘Extremely Lean’ Ethereum, shrinking the chain to near-zero state with ZK proofs
blurb: Vitalik Buterin proposed "Extremely Lean" Ethereum, a two-step redesign of the Beacon Chain that shifts state management to validators and replaces per-epoch balance updates with a single daily ZK-STARK proof, shrinking validator state to roughly 6 bytes. Buterin frames it as Ethereum's third major iteration, on par with the Merge, and says the full "Lean Ethereum" overhaul of consensus and execution could take around four years while scaling the network to millions of validators.

title: Coinbase secures UK investment services license to add derivatives and equities trading
blurb: Coinbase received authorization from the UK Financial Conduct Authority to offer investment services, letting institutional traders access crypto, equity, and commodity perpetual futures while retail users trade equities on the platform for the first time. The license sits alongside Coinbase's existing UK e-money license and crypto registration, and marks its largest UK expansion yet as it builds toward an "everything exchange" spanning payments, savings, derivatives, and equities.

title: Robinhood accelerates global expansion with chain mainnet, stock tokens, agentic trading, and DeFi products
blurb: Robinhood launched the public mainnet of Robinhood Chain, an Arbitrum-based Layer 2 for real-world assets, alongside stock tokens now live in the Robinhood Wallet across more than 120 countries. The tokens support 24/7 trading and can be deployed as DeFi collateral or into lending pools via day-one partners including Uniswap, Chainlink, and BitGo, pushing tokenized equities onto permissionless rails.

title: Where Robinhood's 7% yield comes from
blurb: Robinhood launched Earn, a 7% APY yield product available in the core app to all 27 million users, powered by a Morpho vault that Steakhouse curates across spUSDG, USDe, and SyrupUSDG for overcollateralized institutional lending. Roughly half the yield is marketing incentive today, but as USDG accumulates float, Robinhood expects that share to come from the stablecoin itself. Paired with Robinhood's stock tokens, the setup starts to resemble a decentralized prime broker, insured by Lloyd's of London against smart-contract risk.

title: Klarna seeks U.S. bank charter in latest push beyond buy now, pay later
blurb: Klarna applied to U.S. federal and state regulators to establish Klarna Bank USA, an FDIC-backed institution chartered in Utah, marking its latest move beyond buy now, pay later. Owning a charter would let Klarna fund loans with customer deposits instead of pricier wholesale financing and cut its reliance on partner banks like WebBank, joining Mercury and a wave of fintechs pursuing charters over sponsor-bank arrangements.

title: Coinbase secures UK authorization to offer traditional investments alongside crypto
blurb: Coinbase secured UK regulatory approval to offer equities and derivatives alongside crypto, letting institutional traders access perpetual futures and retail customers trade equities on the platform for the first time. The license advances Coinbase's "Everything Exchange" push and gives it a regulated route to expand beyond crypto ahead of the UK's full crypto framework, expected in October 2027.

title: European banking watchdogs ECB and ESRB warn that frontier AI models pose "systemic risks to the financial system", giving lenders four months to prepare (Financial Times)
blurb: The ECB and ESRB warned that frontier AI models pose systemic risks to the financial system, giving lenders four months to assess and mitigate their exposure. The move signals EU regulators now treat concentrated AI dependence as a stability threat on par with liquidity or credit risk, pressuring banks to inventory model usage and vendor concentration before the deadline.

title: Bitcoin miner TeraWulf soars on a $19 billion AI data-center lease with Anthropic
blurb: TeraWulf signed a 20-year lease with Anthropic to host about 401 megawatts of AI data-center capacity at its Hawesville, Kentucky campus, a deal expected to generate roughly $19 billion in contracted revenue starting in 2027. That figure exceeds the former bitcoin miner's ~$12 billion market value, underscoring how AI hosting contracts are drawing crypto miners away from volatile mining margins toward steadier, single-tenant infrastructure income.

title: ECB Asks Banks for Plans to Address AI Cybersecurity Threats
blurb: The ECB asked eurozone banks to submit plans for defending against cybersecurity threats from frontier AI models like Anthropic's Claude Mythos, escalating supervisory attention on AI-enabled attacks. The request signals that model-driven threats are now a formal prudential concern, putting pressure on banks to document AI security controls the same way they already report on operational resilience and DORA compliance.

title: JPMorgan's JLTXX Tokenized Money Market Fund AUM Grows 250% in a Month on Ethereum
blurb: JPMorgan's JLTXX tokenized money market fund grew its onchain AUM roughly 250% in a month, reaching $695M in TVL seven weeks after a $200M launch, per Token Terminal and Dune data. The fund runs exclusively on Ethereum and is increasingly used as GENIUS Act-compliant reserve backing for stablecoins, joining BlackRock's BUIDL and Superstate's STBXX as banks compete to supply collateral to issuers.

title: Klarna applies for US banking licence
blurb: Klarna applied to the Utah Department of Financial Institutions and the FDIC to charter Klarna Bank, moving from partner-bank arrangements to serving its 30M-plus US customers directly. A charter would let Klarna issue credit and deposit products itself rather than through sponsor banks, following Revolut and Bunq into a US market that Trump-era regulators appear more willing to open to European entrants.

title: Circle’s USDC is leaving Tether behind in the stablecoin volume race, new data from Visa shows
blurb: Circle's USDC accounted for roughly 70% of adjusted stablecoin transaction volume in the first half of 2026, per Visa's onchain dashboard, widening its lead over Tether's USDT at about 25% and reversing USDT's near-90% dominance from 2020. The shift tracks growing bank adoption, with Standard Chartered and BNY building USDC services rather than their own rails, as June volume hit a record $1.79T, up 63% from May.

title: German Savings and Co-op Banks Launch Native Crypto Trading
blurb: Germany's Sparkassen savings banks and Volksbanken cooperative banks are rolling out native cryptocurrency trading inside their existing retail banking apps, giving tens of millions of customers direct access to buy and sell crypto without a third-party exchange. Embedding trading behind trusted incumbent brands pulls retail crypto flows away from standalone exchanges and puts Germany's largest banking networks into direct competition with players like Coinbase and Bitpanda.

title: dYdX Rebrands to Arcus, Partners With Robinhood for Tokenized Stocks
blurb: dYdX rebranded to Arcus and launched a joint product with Robinhood, offering 24/7 trading on 95 tokenized stocks and 35 RWA perps at 0% fees via RFQ execution. The move concedes that its v4 decentralization-first chain lost market share to faster rivals like Hyperliquid, and bets that distribution to Robinhood's 25M users can win it back.

title: Ripple Secures Full MiCA License, Completing EU Compliance
blurb: Ripple received full Crypto Asset Service Provider authorization from Luxembourg's CSSF, completing its MiCA requirements and clearing it to offer regulated crypto payments, custody, and related services across all 30 EEA countries. The approval lands days after MiCA's grandfathering period ended July 1, and pairs with Ripple's existing EU e-money license to put it among the few digital-asset firms with full MiCA authorization.

title: The coin nobody owns
blurb: OpenUSD is a new dollar-backed stablecoin run by an independent company, OpenStandard, backed by Visa, Mastercard, Stripe, Coinbase, BlackRock, and roughly a hundred banks and processors. Unlike Circle and Tether, it shares reserve yield with distributors, keeps governance neutral, and charges no mint-and-burn fees, opening up large-dollar and capital-markets flows that were previously uneconomical. The author argues OpenUSD isn't fighting the incumbents but targeting the money that has stayed on the sidelines.

title: Paradigm leads $5.5 million seed round in M1X Global to expand tokenized sovereign debt platform
blurb: Paradigm led a $5.5M seed round in M1X Global, the firm behind USDM1, the onchain sovereign bond issued with the Republic of the Marshall Islands. The capital will expand M1X's platform for tokenizing sovereign debt, an early test of whether government bonds can move onto public rails and settle with the speed and transparency that traditional fixed-income infrastructure lacks.

title: Galaxy delivers 133 MW of critical IT load to CoreWeave as Helios bitcoin mine turns AI hub
blurb: Galaxy Digital completed Phase I power delivery at its Helios campus in West Texas, handing CoreWeave 133 MW of critical IT load under a 15-year lease and converting a former bitcoin mine into AI infrastructure. CoreWeave has committed to 526 MW across three phases, the site's full approved capacity, projected to generate more than $1B in average annual revenue.

title: Strategy sells 3,588 BTC for $216 million, with total bitcoin holdings still underwater
blurb: Strategy sold 3,588 BTC for $216 million, its first bitcoin sale since adopting an aggressive accumulation strategy, even as its aggregate holdings remain below cost. The company still controls more than 4% of bitcoin's 21 million supply cap, worth roughly $52.3 billion, making its treasury moves a bellwether for corporate bitcoin adoption.

title: Russia's largest bank plans crypto wallet launch as Moscow clears market path
blurb: Sberbank, Russia's largest bank, plans to launch a crypto wallet and digital depository by December, integrated into its Sberbank Online and SberInvestments apps once the "On Digital Currency and Digital Rights" bill takes effect September 1. The move follows years of Bank of Russia opposition and establishes licensed trading, custody, and cross-border settlement, with non-qualified investors capped at roughly 300,000 rubles ($3,800) per year.

title: Plaid talks with banks about potential IPO
blurb: Plaid is in early talks with banks about a potential IPO, though no decision has been made and CFO Seun Sodipo says a listing is not imminent. The move would cap a stretch of aggressive product expansion, including an April 2025 round that valued the account-connectivity firm at $6.1B, down from its 2021 peak, alongside new AI credit-risk models and integrations with Perplexity and Fin.

title: Robinhood launches blockchain designed for real-world assets
blurb: Robinhood launched Robinhood Chain, an Arbitrum-based layer built for tokenized real-world assets and natively connected to its onchain users, with integrations from Alchemy, BitGo, and Chainlink. The chain anchors a broader DeFi push that includes Stock Tokens for 24/7 trading in 120 countries, USDG lending via Robinhood Earn, and agentic crypto accounts in the US.

title: President Trump’s strategic bitcoin reserve hits a legal and jurisdictional snag: Bloomberg
blurb: Trump's plan for a US strategic bitcoin reserve has run into legal and jurisdictional obstacles over how the government can acquire and hold the asset, according to Bloomberg. The snag raises questions about whether the reserve can proceed under existing executive authority or will require congressional action, leaving the timeline and funding mechanism unresolved.

title: Crypto Card Spending Surpasses $800M Monthly
blurb: Crypto-linked debit and credit card spending crossed $800M per month, as issuers like Visa and Mastercard route stablecoin and token balances onto traditional card rails. The volume signals that on-chain funds are increasingly spent at ordinary merchants rather than held, pushing card networks and their fintech program managers to build settlement and compliance flows that bridge crypto wallets with existing interchange economics.

title: Russia’s largest bank Sber targets early December crypto wallet launch: report
blurb: Sberbank, Russia's largest bank, plans to launch a crypto wallet and digital depository inside its Sber apps by early December, pending final passage of Russia's new digital asset law. The move would put crypto custody directly inside a state-controlled banking app serving millions, signaling that Russian financial institutions expect regulatory cover to offer digital-asset services domestically.

title: Strategy Sells 3,588 Bitcoin for $216M to Fund Dividend Payments
blurb: Strategy sold 3,588 Bitcoin for roughly $216M between June 29 and July 5, using the proceeds to fund preferred-stock dividends and replenish its USD reserve, per an 8-K filed with the SEC. The sale funded distributions on its STRF, STRE, STRK, STRD and STRC securities directly, leaving the full $1.25B capacity of its new Bitcoin Monetization Program untouched and its holdings at 843,775 BTC.

title: Bitcoin's U.S. reserve still a work-in-progress as federal agencies hash it out
blurb: The White House said it is still evaluating the "best structure" for a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve and a separate digital-asset stockpile, sixteen months after Trump's March 2025 executive order kicked off the effort, with Treasury and Commerce both lobbying to house the fund. Advisers concede the reserve needs congressional legislation to activate, and no bill has advanced, leaving the plan stalled ahead of midterms that could flip the House.

title: Judge revives fraud claim against Barry Silbert, DCG in Genesis Yield lawsuit
blurb: A federal judge revived a fraud claim against DCG founder Barry Silbert and the firm in a class action over Genesis Yield, the lending program that paid customers interest on deposited crypto. The ruling lets investors pursue allegations that DCG and Silbert misrepresented Genesis's financial health before the lender collapsed, keeping litigation risk alive for one of crypto's largest holding companies.

title: Securitize eyes acquisitions with $400 million war chest after going public, CEO says
blurb: Securitize plans to deploy the $400M it raised through its Cantor-backed SPAC merger and NYSE debut to acquire businesses that complement its institutional tokenization platform, CEO Carlos Domingo told CoinDesk, ruling out buying rivals. The firm has issued roughly $4.4B in tokenized assets for clients including BlackRock, Apollo, and KKR, and is betting on tokenized equities and ETFs as the next growth wave.

title: DeFi protocol Summer.fi halts Lazy Summer vaults after $6 million exploit
blurb: Summer.fi paused all Lazy Summer Protocol vaults after a flash-loan attack manipulated the accounting logic of its automated USDC vaults, draining roughly $6 million from a platform that held $22M in total value locked. The exploit, which routed a Morpho-sourced flash loan to inflate and redeem assets, sent the SUMR token down more than 18% and underscores the accounting-logic risk in automated yield protocols that rebalance across Aave and Morpho.

title: Kraken platform Payward acquires payments firm Reap
blurb: Payward, the parent company of Kraken, completed its acquisition of payments firm Reap to expand its Payward Services B2B platform with embedded card issuance, cross-border payments, and stablecoin-based treasury management. The deal gives partners access to Payward's liquidity, custody, and settlement infrastructure through a single API, positioning stablecoin settlement as an alternative to correspondent banks and regional processors for cross-border money movement.

title: Apple Pay will let shoppers check out using American Express points
blurb: Apple and American Express added a feature to Apple Pay that lets cardholders redeem Amex Membership Rewards points directly at checkout, both online and in stores, without leaving the payment flow. The integration moves points redemption from a separate portal into the tap-to-pay moment, giving Amex a way to drive engagement while making rewards a more visible competitor to cash and credit at the point of sale.

title: Pay for APIs with USDC via x402 and Apify
blurb: Apify integrated the x402 payment protocol, letting developers pay for its web-scraping APIs with USDC per request instead of maintaining a prepaid account or subscription. The setup revives HTTP's 402 Payment Required status to settle stablecoin micropayments inline with each API call, pointing toward pay-per-use machine-to-machine billing where agents can transact without card rails or human-managed credentials.

title: President Trump says he’s a ‘big crypto guy,’ while distancing himself from family’s crypto ventures
blurb: President Trump called cryptocurrency "powerful" and described himself as a "big crypto guy" at the launch of Trump investment accounts for children, while publicly distancing himself from his family's crypto ventures. The rhetorical split lets the administration champion favorable crypto policy and market structure legislation while insulating the White House from conflict-of-interest scrutiny over ventures like World Liberty Financial.

title: Ripple's preliminary crypto asset provider license in Luxembourg upgraded to fully compliant
blurb: Ripple received a full MiCA Crypto-Asset Service Provider license from Luxembourg's CSSF, clearing it to passport regulated cryptoasset services across all 30 European Economic Area countries. The full CASP authorization, which builds on Ripple's February EMI approval, places it among a small group of firms compliant in the post-transitional MiCA era while unlicensed competitors like Binance are forced to halt EEA operations.

title: Amazon Returns to US Bond Market to Fund AI Infrastructure Build
blurb: Amazon returned to the US investment-grade bond market to help finance its AI infrastructure buildout, tapping debt investors as capital spending on data centers and compute accelerates. The move signals that hyperscalers increasingly view bond issuance, rather than cash flow alone, as the funding mechanism for the AI capex cycle, adding fresh supply to a corporate debt market already absorbing heavy tech borrowing.

title: Wall Street Buys Into Musk’s SpaceX Vision
blurb: SpaceX is joining the Nasdaq 100 Index, and analysts at major global brokers are now recommending investors buy shares of Musk's rocket and satellite venture. The index inclusion forces passive funds to hold SpaceX and signals that Wall Street is treating the still-private-adjacent space company as a mainstream large-cap holding rather than a speculative bet.

title: Strategy selling hundreds of millions worth of bitcoin raises question about its capital-allocation playbook
blurb: Strategy sold 3,588 BTC for roughly $216M just days after buying 3,657 BTC at higher prices, and disclosed an $8.31B unrealized loss on its holdings for the second quarter as bitcoin fell from about $68,000 to $60,000. The selling appears aimed at protecting dividends on its high-yielding Stretch preferred stock, raising questions about whether the accumulate-and-hold treasury playbook holds up when prices fall.

title: Bitmine added another $74 million in ether as Tom Lee bets on Clarity Act boost
blurb: Bitmine Immersion bought another 42,197 ether last week, worth roughly $74 million, lifting its stash to 5.74 million ETH, or 4.8% of circulating supply, as chairman Tom Lee tied ETH's outperformance to expectations that the Clarity Act will pass. The accumulation contrasts with Strategy, which sold about $216 million in bitcoin to raise cash amid dividend obligations and the market downturn.

title: Michael Saylor's Strategy dramatically ups pace of bitcoin sales, raising $216 million
blurb: Strategy sold 3,588 bitcoin for roughly $216 million last week at an average price near $60,000, cutting its holdings to 843,775 BTC and funneling proceeds into the $2.55 billion dollar reserve it uses to pay preferred-stock dividends. The pace marks a sharp escalation from the 32 BTC it sold a month earlier, signaling that Strategy is now leaning on treasury sales, not equity issuance, to cover its distributions.

title: With AI, a wrong answer is a bug. A wrong action is an incident
blurb: The author argues that as banks push AI agents into production, the security risk shifts from what a model can see to what an agent is allowed to do inside systems that move money and hold regulated data. A copilot's wrong answer is a quality bug, but an autonomous agent's wrong action is an incident, sometimes reportable. On April 30, the Five Eyes cyber agencies issued their first joint guidance on agentic AI, naming overprivileged agents the leading risk and calling strict least privilege critical.

title: World Cup sends prediction market volumes soaring to record highs
blurb: Prediction-market platforms posted record trading volumes in June as the 2026 FIFA World Cup drove speculation, with Kalshi clearing more than $31B in notional volume, up over 70% from May, and Polymarket's international exchange topping $10.8B. The surge extended to new entrants like Rothera, the Susquehanna-Robinhood venture that captured 7% of U.S. prediction-market volume in its debut month.

title: Upbit, Naver stock-swap deal faces second delay amid proposed crypto law debate
blurb: Naver Financial and Dunamu, Upbit's parent, pushed their $9.9B stock swap to Dec. 31, 2026, the second delay to a deal that would fold South Korea's largest crypto exchange into Naver's financial network. The companies cited the pending Digital Asset Basic Act, which is debating a 20% cap on how much a major shareholder can hold in a domestic exchange, as a factor that could reshape or void the agreement.

title: Square introduces ChatGPT and Claude integrations for AI-powered discovery
blurb: Square launched a ChatGPT app and Claude plugin that let U.S. Food & Beverage sellers get discovered and take orders directly inside AI conversations, routing through existing Square Online Ordering, POS, and Kitchen Display setups via Order by Cash App. Eligible sellers are opted in with no setup or API work, and Square charges no added marketplace commission, a pointed contrast to the margin-compressing fees on traditional delivery platforms.

title: Binance taps into Bitcoin holders’ hunger for yield with new covered call yield play
blurb: Binance launched BTC Yield, a covered-call product inside Binance Earn that accepts only bitcoin deposits and systematically writes BTC call options, distributing most collected premiums to users as weekly BTC payouts and retained value in their BTCY position. The launch brings a strategy that once demanded options expertise to retail holders, and arrives as BlackRock rolls out a similar covered-call bitcoin income ETF.

title: Tencent Sells $1.5 Billion Kuaishou Stake as AI Pivot Quickens
blurb: Tencent sold $1.5B of its stake in short-video platform Kuaishou, trimming a mature internet holding as it redirects capital toward AI infrastructure and model development. The sale signals a broader capital-allocation shift among China's platform giants, who are unwinding legacy consumer-internet positions to fund the compute and talent costs of competing in AI.

title: Nvidia Supplier Unimicron Raises $1.4 Billion in GDS Sale
blurb: Unimicron Technology raised $1.36B through a sale of global depositary shares, riding an AI-driven rally that has lifted the Nvidia supplier's stock more than 700% over the past 12 months. The Taiwanese chipmaker's raise shows how AI demand is now pulling capital deep into the hardware supply chain, letting component makers tap public markets on the same enthusiasm inflating chip designers' valuations.

title: UK regulator warns of "arms race" to keep up with AI use in financial services
blurb: The FCA's Sheldon Mills warned that regulators are in an "arms race" to keep pace with AI in financial services, calling for expanded powers and a three-to-six-month review of whether consumer use of ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini for financial decisions falls outside the regulatory perimeter. Research he commissioned found a fifth of UK adults are open to letting AI models make savings or borrowing decisions, despite having no recourse to compensation when unregulated advice goes wrong.
